#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
OmegA — Sovereign Intelligence CLI
R · W · Ϝ · Y
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession
from prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest import AutoSuggestFromHistory
from prompt_toolkit.formatted_text import HTML
from prompt_toolkit.history import FileHistory
from prompt_toolkit.key_binding import KeyBindings
from prompt_toolkit.styles import Style
from rich import box
from rich.columns import Columns
from rich.console import Console
from rich.live import Live
from rich.markdown import Markdown
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.rule import Rule
from rich.syntax import Syntax
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text
from rich.theme import Theme
from rich.tree import Tree

# ── OmegA CLI modules ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
try:
    from omega_cli import monitoring, audit, utils
except ImportError:
    monitoring = None
    audit = None
    utils = None

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENV_FILES = [
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "one-true.env",
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "gateway.env",
]
READLINE_HISTORY = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "readline_history"
SESSION_LOG      = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "history.jsonl"
SESSIONS_DIR     = Path.home() / ".omega" / "sessions"
API_BASE         = os.environ.get("OMEGA_API_URL", "https://omega-sovereign.vercel.app")

for _p in [READLINE_HISTORY.parent, SESSIONS_DIR]:
    _p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── Risk classification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

READONLY_CMDS   = {"ls","ll","la","cat","head","tail","echo","pwd","env","printenv",
                   "which","file","find","grep","rg","fd","wc","diff","stat","tree",
                   "git status","git log","git diff","git show","git branch",
                   "ps","top","df","du","free","uname","whoami","id","date","uptime"}
HIGHRISK_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b(rm|rmdir|mv|cp|chmod|chown|sudo|dd|mkfs|fdisk|parted|"
    r"git\s+push|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+rebase|git\s+force|"
    r"pip\s+install|npm\s+install|apt\s+install|apt-get)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE
)

def classify_risk(cmd: str) -> str:
    base = cmd.strip().split()[0] if cmd.strip() else ""
    if any(cmd.strip().startswith(r) for r in READONLY_CMDS):
        return "readonly"
    if HIGHRISK_PATTERNS.search(cmd):
        return "high"
    return "standard"

# ── Theme ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

THEME = Theme({
    "omega":   "bold #a855f7",
    "user":    "bold #22d4f5",
    "dim":     "dim #4a4947",
    "gold":    "bold #e8af34",
    "green":   "#6bcf7f",
    "red":     "bold #ff6b6b",
    "yellow":  "#ffd93d",
    "muted":   "#797876",
    "border":  "#2a2826",
    "banner":  "bold #22d4f5",
    "high":    "bold #ff6b6b",
    "std":     "#ffd93d",
    "low":     "#6bcf7f",
    "code":    "#22d4f5",
})

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

PROMPT_STYLE = Style.from_dict({
    "prompt":       "#22d4f5 bold",
    "prompt.arrow": "#a855f7 bold",
    "":             "#cdccca",
})

# ── Env ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def load_env():
    for path in ENV_FILES:
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            k, v = line.split("=", 1)
            os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip('"').strip("'"))

# ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def new_session_id() -> str:
    return f"Ω-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d-%H%M')}"

def session_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return SESSIONS_DIR / f"{sid}.json"

def save_session(sid: str, history: list, state: dict):
    data = {
        "session_id": sid,
        "created_at": state.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
        "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "messages": history,
        "state": state,
    }
    path = session_path(sid)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load_session(sid: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    path = session_path(sid)
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_sessions() -> list:
    sessions = []
    for p in sorted(SESSIONS_DIR.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:20]:
        try:
            d = json.loads(p.read_text())
            sessions.append(d)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return sessions

# ── Git context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def git_info() -> str:
    try:
        branch = subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True
        ).strip()
        dirty = subprocess.call(
            ["git", "diff", "--quiet"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
        ) != 0
        return f"{branch}{'*' if dirty else ''}"
    except Exception:
        return ""

# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_banner(session_id: str):
    console.print()
    # Colorshifting banner — cycles through user's terminal colors
    colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", "#f97316", "#eab308", "#10b981", "#22d4f5"]
    banner_lines = [
        "██████╗     ██╗    ██╗   ███████╗    ██╗   ██╗",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║    ██║   ██╔════╝    ╚██╗ ██╔╝",
        "██████╔╝    ██║ █╗ ██║   █████╗       ╚████╔╝ ",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║███╗██║   ██╔══╝        ╚██╔╝  ",
        "██║  ██║    ╚███╔███╔╝   ██║            ██║   ",
        "╚═╝  ╚═╝    ╚══╝╚══╝    ╚═╝            ╚═╝   ",
    ]
    for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):
        color = colors[i % len(colors)]
        console.print(f"  [{color}]{line}[/{color}]")
    console.print()

    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    mode_color = "green" if token else "muted"
    mode_label = "operator" if token else "public"
    git = git_info()
    git_str = f"  [muted]git:[/muted][dim]{git}[/dim]" if git else ""
    cwd_str = f"  [muted]cwd:[/muted][dim]{Path.cwd().name}[/dim]"

    console.print(
        f"  [gold]R · W · Ϝ · Y[/gold]   [{mode_color}]{mode_label}[/{mode_color}]"
        f"  [muted]session:[/muted][dim]{session_id}[/dim]{cwd_str}{git_str}"
    )
    console.print(
        "  [muted]/help for commands  ·  /new to clear  ·  ctrl+c to exit  ·  "
        "!cmd to run shell[/muted]"
    )
    console.print()

# ── Response state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

last_state: dict = {}

# ── Streaming chat ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def stream_response(user: str, history: list, inject_context: str = "") -> str:
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "x-omega-operator-token": token,
    }
    msg = f"{inject_context}\n\n{user}".strip() if inject_context else user
    payload = {"user": msg, "history": history, "voiceMode": False}

    full_response = ""

    with console.status("[muted]connecting…[/muted]", spinner="dots",
                        spinner_style="#a855f7") as status:
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120) as client:
                async with client.stream(
                    "POST", f"{API_BASE}/api/chat",
                    headers=headers, json=payload,
                ) as resp:
                    # Capture response headers for /state
                    global last_state
                    last_state = {
                        "mode":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Mode", "?"),
                        "provider":   resp.headers.get("X-Provider", "?"),
                        "risk":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Risk-Score", "?"),
                        "outcome":    resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Outcome", "?"),
                        "session_id": resp.headers.get("X-Session-Id", "?"),
                        "envelope":   resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Envelope-Version", "?"),
                        "phylactery": resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Phylactery-Head", "?"),
                        "stack":      resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Stack", "?"),
                    }

                    if resp.status_code != 200:
                        body = await resp.aread()
                        body_text = body.decode()
                        status.stop()

                        # Better error diagnostics
                        if resp.status_code == 500:
                            console.print(f"\n[red]  error 500: internal server error[/red]")
                            payload_size = len(json.dumps(payload))
                            if payload_size > 100000:  # ~100KB
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  hint: request size ~{payload_size//1024}KB (too large)[/yellow]")
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  try: split your prompt into smaller pieces[/yellow]")
                            else:
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  hint: {body_text[:200]}[/yellow]")
                        else:
                            console.print(f"\n[red]  error {resp.status_code}: {body_text[:200]}[/red]\n")
                        return ""

                    status.update("[muted]synthesizing…[/muted]")

                    buf = ""
                    async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                        buf += chunk
                        while "\n" in buf:
                            line, buf = buf.split("\n", 1)
                            if line.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                                continue
                            if line.strip():
                                full_response += line + "\n"
                    if buf.strip() and not buf.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                        full_response += buf

        except httpx.ConnectError:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  connection error — {API_BASE} unreachable[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except httpx.TimeoutException:
            status.stop()
            console.print("\n[red]  request timed out[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except Exception as e:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  error: {e}[/red]\n")
            return ""

    if not full_response.strip():
        return ""

    _render_response(full_response.strip())
    return full_response.strip()


def _render_response(text: str, history: list = None):
    console.print()
    is_markdown = any(m in text for m in ("**", "##", "```", "\n- ", "\n* ", "\n1. ", "> "))
    if is_markdown:
        console.print(Panel(
            Markdown(text),
            border_style="#2a2826",
            padding=(0, 2),
            title="[omega]Ω[/omega]",
            title_align="left",
        ))
    else:
        # Wrap long plain text
        console.print(f"[omega]Ω ›[/omega]  {text}")
    console.print()

    # Show smart suggestions (if available and history provided)
    if utils and history:
        try:
            suggestions = utils.get_smart_suggestions(text, history)
            if suggestions:
                console.print("[dim]Suggestions:[/dim]")
                for i, s in enumerate(suggestions, 1):
                    console.print(
                        f"  {i}. [{s['type']}] {s['label']:<30} "
                        f"[dim]— {s['description']}[/dim]"
                    )
                console.print()
        except Exception:
            pass  # Suggestions are optional

# ── Shell command execution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def run_shell_command(cmd: str) -> bool:
    """Execute a bang-prefixed command with risk gate. Returns True if executed."""
    risk = classify_risk(cmd)

    if risk == "high":
        console.print(f"\n[high]  ⚠  HIGH RISK:[/high] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]This operation is destructive. Continue? [y/N][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer != "y":
            console.print("  [muted]  blocked.[/muted]\n")
            return False
    elif risk == "standard":
        console.print(f"\n[std]  ▸ standard:[/std] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]Execute? [Y/n][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer == "n":
            console.print("  [muted]  skipped.[/muted]\n")
            return False

    console.print()
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True,
                                capture_output=False)
        if result.returncode != 0:
            console.print(f"  [muted]exit code {result.returncode}[/muted]")
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]  shell error: {e}[/red]")
    console.print()
    return True

# ── Slash commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def cmd_help():
    console.print()
    console.print(Rule("[muted]commands[/muted]", style="#2a2826"))
    rows = [
        ("/new",           "clear conversation history"),
        ("/exit /quit",    "quit"),
        ("/state",         "show last response state (mode, provider, risk, outcome)"),
        ("/chain",         "show Phylactery info from last response"),
        ("/sessions",      "list saved sessions"),
        ("/resume [id]",   "resume a previous session"),
        ("/save",          "save current session now"),
        ("/read <path>",   "read a file and add to next message context"),
        ("/search <q>",    "grep codebase for a pattern"),
        ("/files [path]",  "browse file tree"),
        ("/dashboard",     "system status"),
        ("/history",       "show current conversation turns"),
        ("/help",          "this menu"),
        ("",               ""),
        ("!<cmd>",         "run shell command (with risk gate)"),
        ("ctrl+l",         "clear screen and reprint banner"),
        ("ctrl+r",         "search input history"),
        ("↑ / ↓",          "scroll input history"),
    ]
    t = Table(box=None, padding=(0, 2), show_header=False)
    t.add_column(style="user", no_wrap=True)
    t.add_column(style="muted")
    for cmd, desc in rows:
        t.add_row(cmd, desc)
    console.print(t)
    console.print(Rule(style="#2a2826"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_state():
    if not last_state:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no state yet — send a message first[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    t = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, border_style="#2a2826", show_header=False, padding=(0, 1))
    t.add_column(style="muted", no_wrap=True)
    t.add_column(style="dim")
    for k, v in last_state.items():
        t.add_row(k, str(v))
    console.print(Panel(t, title="[omega]state vector[/omega]",
                        border_style="#2a2826", title_align="left"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_chain():
    if not last_state:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no chain data yet[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    ph = last_state.get("phylactery", "?")
    ev = last_state.get("envelope", "?")
    console.print(Panel(
        f"[muted]phylactery head:[/muted] [dim]{ph}[/dim]\n"
        f"[muted]envelope version:[/muted] [dim]{ev}[/dim]\n"
        f"[muted]stack:[/muted] [dim]{last_state.get('stack','?')}[/dim]",
        title="[omega]Ω chain[/omega]",
        border_style="#2a2826",
        title_align="left",
    ))
    console.print()


async def cmd_sessions():
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    t = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, border_style="#2a2826",
              title="[omega]saved sessions[/omega]", title_justify="left")
    t.add_column("session id", style="user", no_wrap=True)
    t.add_column("turns", style="dim", justify="right")
    t.add_column("updated", style="muted")
    t.add_column("model", style="dim")
    for s in sessions:
        turns = len(s.get("messages", [])) // 2
        updated = s.get("updated_at", "")[:16].replace("T", " ")
        model = s.get("state", {}).get("provider", "?")
        t.add_row(s.get("session_id", "?"), str(turns), updated, model)
    console.print(t)
    console.print()
    return sessions


async def cmd_resume(args: str, current_session_id: str, history: list, state: dict):
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions to resume[/muted]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    if args:
        sid = args.strip()
    else:
        # Show picker
        await cmd_sessions()
        console.print("  [muted]enter session id to resume (or press enter to cancel):[/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            sid = input().strip()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            sid = ""
        if not sid:
            return current_session_id, history, state

    data = load_session(sid)
    if not data:
        # Try partial match
        for s in sessions:
            if sid in s.get("session_id", ""):
                data = s
                sid = s["session_id"]
                break

    if not data:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  session not found: {sid}[/red]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    loaded_history = data.get("messages", [])
    loaded_state   = data.get("state", {})
    loaded_state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat())

    console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ resumed:[/green] [dim]{sid}[/dim]  "
                  f"[muted]({len(loaded_history)//2} turns)[/muted]\n")
    # Show last 2 turns
    for msg in loaded_history[-4:]:
        role_tag = "[user]you ›[/user]" if msg["role"] == "user" else "[omega]Ω ›[/omega]"
        preview = msg["text"][:200].replace("\n", " ")
        console.print(f"  {role_tag}  [dim]{preview}…[/dim]")
    console.print()

    return sid, loaded_history, loaded_state


async def cmd_read(args: str) -> str:
    """Read a file and return its content as context injection."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /read <path>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    path = Path(args.strip()).expanduser()
    if not path.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  file not found: {path}[/red]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        content = path.read_text(errors="replace")
        size = len(content)
        if size > 50_000:
            content = content[:50_000] + "\n… (truncated)"
        # Detect language for syntax highlight
        suffix = path.suffix.lstrip(".")
        lang_map = {"py": "python", "ts": "typescript", "tsx": "tsx",
                    "js": "javascript", "rs": "rust", "go": "go",
                    "json": "json", "md": "markdown", "sh": "bash",
                    "toml": "toml", "yaml": "yaml", "yml": "yaml",
                    "css": "css", "html": "html", "sql": "sql"}
        lang = lang_map.get(suffix, "text")
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            Syntax(content[:3000], lang, theme="monokai",
                   line_numbers=True, word_wrap=True)
            if lang != "text" else Text(content[:3000], style="dim"),
            title=f"[code]{path.name}[/code]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
            subtitle=f"[muted]{size:,} chars — injecting into next message[/muted]",
        ))
        console.print()
        return f"[File: {path}]\n```{lang}\n{content}\n```"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  read error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_search(args: str) -> str:
    """Grep the codebase and optionally inject results."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /search <pattern>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             "--include=*.go", "--include=*.md",
             "-l", args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        files = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
        if not files:
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  no matches for:[/muted] {args}\n")
            return ""

        # Show matches
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            "\n".join(f"[code]{f}[/code]" for f in files[:30]),
            title=f"[omega]search: {args}[/omega]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
        ))
        console.print()

        # Get actual line matches (first 20)
        result2 = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        matches = result2.stdout.strip()[:3000]
        return f"[Search results for '{args}']\n{matches}"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  search error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_files(args: str):
    base = Path(args.strip()).expanduser() if args.strip() else Path.cwd()
    if not base.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  path not found: {base}[/red]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    tree = Tree(f"[code]{base.name}/[/code]", guide_style="#2a2826")

    def add_children(node, path: Path, depth: int = 0):
        if depth > 3:
            return
        try:
            items = sorted(path.iterdir(), key=lambda x: (x.is_file(), x.name))
        except PermissionError:
            return
        shown = 0
        for item in items:
            if item.name.startswith(".") or item.name in (
                "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".git", "dist", ".next",
                "venv", ".venv", "target", "build"
            ):
                continue
            if shown >= 20:
                node.add("[muted]…[/muted]")
                break
            if item.is_dir():
                branch = node.add(f"[gold]{item.name}/[/gold]")
                add_children(branch, item, depth + 1)
            else:
                size = item.stat().st_size
                size_str = f"[muted]{size:,}b[/muted]" if size < 10000 else f"[muted]{size//1024}kb[/muted]"
                node.add(f"[dim]{item.name}[/dim]  {size_str}")
            shown += 1

    add_children(tree, base)
    console.print(Panel(tree, border_style="#2a2826",
                        title=f"[code]{base}[/code]", title_align="left"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_dashboard(session_id: str, history: list):
    console.print()
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    mode = "operator" if token else "public"
    git  = git_info()

    # System info
    try:
        import psutil
        cpu  = psutil.cpu_percent(interval=0.1)
        ram  = psutil.virtual_memory()
        ram_str = f"{ram.used // 1024**2:,}MB / {ram.total // 1024**2:,}MB ({ram.percent:.0f}%)"
        cpu_str = f"{cpu:.0f}%"
    except ImportError:
        cpu_str = "n/a"
        ram_str = "n/a"

    info = Table(box=None, padding=(0, 2), show_header=False)
    info.add_column(style="muted", no_wrap=True)
    info.add_column(style="dim")
    info.add_row("session",  session_id)
    info.add_row("mode",     mode)
    info.add_row("endpoint", API_BASE)
    info.add_row("git",      git or "none")
    info.add_row("cwd",      str(Path.cwd()))
    info.add_row("turns",    str(len(history) // 2))
    info.add_row("cpu",      cpu_str)
    info.add_row("ram",      ram_str)
    if last_state:
        info.add_row("provider", last_state.get("provider", "?"))
        info.add_row("risk",     last_state.get("risk", "?"))
        info.add_row("outcome",  last_state.get("outcome", "?"))

    console.print(Panel(info, title="[omega]Ω dashboard[/omega]",
                        border_style="#2a2826", title_align="left"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_history_show(history: list):
    if not history:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no history yet[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    for i in range(0, len(history), 2):
        user_msg  = history[i]["text"] if i < len(history) else ""
        omega_msg = history[i+1]["text"] if i+1 < len(history) else ""
        console.print(f"  [dim]{i//2 + 1}.[/dim] [user]you ›[/user] "
                      f"[dim]{user_msg[:100]}[/dim]")
        if omega_msg:
            console.print(f"     [omega]Ω ›[/omega] "
                          f"[dim]{omega_msg[:100].replace(chr(10),' ')}…[/dim]")
        console.print()

# ── Key bindings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def make_bindings() -> KeyBindings:
    kb = KeyBindings()

    @kb.add("c-l")
    def _clear(event):
        event.app.renderer.reset()
        os.system("clear")

    return kb

# ── REPL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def repl(resume_id: Optional[str] = None):
    session: PromptSession = PromptSession(
        history=FileHistory(str(READLINE_HISTORY)),
        auto_suggest=AutoSuggestFromHistory(),
        style=PROMPT_STYLE,
        key_bindings=make_bindings(),
        enable_history_search=True,
        mouse_support=False,
    )

    session_id = resume_id or new_session_id()
    history: list = []
    state: dict   = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}

    # Resume if requested
    if resume_id:
        data = load_session(resume_id)
        if data:
            history = data.get("messages", [])
            state   = data.get("state", state)
            state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", state["created_at"])

    pending_context = ""   # injected by /read or /search
    save_counter    = 0

    print_banner(session_id)

    while True:
        try:
            line = await session.prompt_async(
                HTML("<prompt>you</prompt> <prompt.arrow>›</prompt.arrow> ")
            )
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved → {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue

        # ── Bang commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if line.startswith("!"):
            await run_shell_command(line[1:].strip())
            continue

        # ── Slash commands ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        parts = line.split(None, 1)
        cmd   = parts[0].lower()
        args  = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""

        if cmd in ("/exit", "/quit", "exit", "quit"):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved → {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        if cmd == "/new":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            history.clear()
            pending_context = ""
            session_id = new_session_id()
            state = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  new session:[/muted] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/help":
            await cmd_help()
            continue

        if cmd == "/state":
            await cmd_state()
            continue

        if cmd == "/chain":
            await cmd_chain()
            continue

        if cmd == "/sessions":
            await cmd_sessions()
            continue

        if cmd == "/resume":
            session_id, history, state = await cmd_resume(
                args, session_id, history, state
            )
            continue

        if cmd == "/save":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ saved:[/green] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/read":
            pending_context = await cmd_read(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]file loaded — it will be injected into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/search":
            pending_context = await cmd_search(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]results loaded — injecting into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/files":
            await cmd_files(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/dashboard":
            await cmd_dashboard(session_id, history)
            continue

        if cmd in ("/history", "/log"):
            await cmd_history_show(history)
            continue

        # ── /monitor command ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd.startswith("/monitor"):
            if monitoring:
                console.print("\n[cyan]Starting real-time monitoring (Ctrl+C to stop)...[/cyan]\n")
                db1_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB_URL", "")
                db2_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB2_URL", "")
                try:
                    monitoring.monitor_live(db1_url, db2_url, API_BASE, interval=10)
                except Exception as e:
                    console.print(f"\n[red]Error during monitoring: {e}[/red]\n")
                # Log to ERGON
                if audit:
                    audit.log_cli_action("/monitor", exit_code=0, duration_sec=0)
            else:
                console.print("\n[red]Monitoring module not available[/red]\n")
            continue

        # ── /help command ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd in ("/help", "/h", "/?"):
            console.print("\n[cyan]Available commands:[/cyan]")
            console.print("  /monitor              Show real-time DB and API status")
            console.print("  /new                  Start a new session")
            console.print("  /sessions             List saved sessions")
            console.print("  /state                Show last response state")
            console.print("  !cmd                  Run shell command")
            console.print("  Ctrl+C                Exit\n")
            continue

        # ── Unknown slash command ───────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd.startswith("/"):
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  unknown command: {cmd}  (try /help)[/muted]\n")
            continue

        # ── Send to OmegA ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        inject  = pending_context
        pending_context = ""

        reply = await stream_response(line, history, inject_context=inject)

        if reply:
            history.append({"role": "user",  "text": line})
            history.append({"role": "omega", "text": reply})
            state["provider"] = last_state.get("provider", "?")

            # Auto-save every 10 turns
            save_counter += 1
            if save_counter % 10 == 0:
                save_session(session_id, history, state)

# ── One-shot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def one_shot(prompt: str) -> int:
    reply = await stream_response(prompt, [])
    return 0 if reply else 1

# ── Entry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    load_env()
    args = sys.argv[1:]

    if not args:
        if not sys.stdin.isatty():
            prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
            if prompt:
                return asyncio.run(one_shot(prompt))
        asyncio.run(repl())
        return 0

    if args[0] in ("-h", "--help", "help"):
        console.print("[user]omega[/user]                    interactive REPL")
        console.print('[user]omega "question"[/user]         one-shot query')
        console.print("[user]omega resume[/user]             pick a session to resume")
        console.print("[user]omega resume Ω-2026-...[/user]  resume specific session")
        return 0

    if args[0] == "resume":
        sid = args[1] if len(args) > 1 else None
        asyncio.run(repl(resume_id=sid))
        return 0

    return asyncio.run(one_shot(" ".join(args)))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        sys.exit(main())
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        console.print("\n[muted]  goodbye.[/muted]\n")
        sys.exit(0)

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user explicitly requested three concurrent deliverables: (1) Resume the stalled INBOX ingest pipeline (~2,400 items remaining from the original 3,593) with real-time DB2 utilization monitoring, (2) Debug the omega CLI that was crashing with "error 500: internal error" when the user tried to generate a comprehensive upgrade prompt, and (3) Implement a colorshifting banner matching their terminal's visual style and provide detailed documentation for comprehensive CLI enhancements. The user specified the exact ASCII banner they wanted, emphasizing the colorshifting capability.

2. Key Technical Concepts:

   - Shadow Workflow Generation: Analysis of agent execution patterns and task completion to identify optimized tool sequences
   - CLI Enhancement Architecture: Modular Python package design with graceful fallback for optional features
   - ERGON.md Audit Logging: Append-only execution log with SHA256 checksum integrity verification
   - Rich Terminal Rendering: Using the Rich library for dynamic tables, panels, and live-updating dashboards
   - Colorshifting UI Elements: Cycling through multiple terminal color codes (#22d4f5, #a855f7, #ec4899, #f97316, #eab308, #10b981) to create visually dynamic banners

3. Files and Code Sections:
   
   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/INGEST/resume_sweep2_with_monitoring.sh` (11 KB, CREATED)
     - Purpose: Resumable INBOX ingest script with real-time DB2 monitoring

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega` (MODIFIED - Primary CLI Script)
     - Modified colorshifting banner implementation:
       ```python
       def print_banner(session_id: str):
           console.print()
           colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", "#f97316", "#eab308", "#10b981", "#22d4f5"]

               "██╔══██╗    ██║███╗██║   ██╔══╝        ╚██╔╝  ",
               "██║  ██║    ╚███╔███╔╝   ██║            ██║   ",
               "╚═╝  ╚═╝    ╚══╝╚══╝    ╚═╝            ╚═╝   ",
           ]
           for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):
               color = colors[i % len(colors)]
               console.print(f"  [{color}]{line}[/{color}]")
       ```

       ```python

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega_cli/monitoring.py` (200 lines, CREATED)
     - Purpose: Real-time database and API health monitoring

     - Result: Each line of banner shifts to next color, creating rainbow effect
   
   - Error: Attempting to paste all 4 parts of the comprehensive upgrade prompt at once was likely to trigger error 500 due to size
     - Fix: Split comprehensive prompt into 4 manageable parts (5.6–11 KB each) with explicit instructions to paste one part at a time, waiting for response between parts

   The work being performed immediately before the summary request was autonomous execution of the dual-workstream implementation that the user approved. Specifically:
   
   - **Workstream 1 (Ingest)**: Started `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/INGEST/resume_sweep2_with_monitoring.sh` in background (PID 463051). The script successfully:

   - **Workstream 2 (CLI Upgrades)**: Implemented Phase 1 of CLI enhancements:
     - Created `omega_cli/` package with three new modules (monitoring.py, audit.py, utils.py)

# OmegA CLI Enhancement Prompt (Part 2/4)

## Phase 3: Advanced Features

### 3.1 Real-Time DB & System Monitoring
**Goal**: Built-in dashboard for DB status, ingest progress, API health.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/monitor` command that shows live metrics in a panel:
  ```
  ┌─ OmegA Status Dashboard ─────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ DB1 (omega-personal):    ████████░░ 87.3% (446 MB / 512 MB)  │
  │ DB2 (omega-technical):   ███░░░░░░ 47.3% (242 MB / 512 MB)   │
  │ Memory entries:          26,143 (avg importance: 0.82)        │
  │ API health:              ✓ OK (latency: 142ms)                │
  │ Session age:             3h 24m (tokens: 12.5K / $0.047)      │
  │                                                                │
  │ [↑ Ingest] [Expand DB] [Export] [Settings]                   │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  ```
- Use `rich.live.Live` for real-time updates
- Add `/monitor --interval 5` for custom refresh rate
- Add `/monitor --export-csv` to save metrics

### 3.2 File-Based Prompt Management
**Goal**: Handle large prompts by saving to file and referencing instead of embedding in JSON.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/prompt-file <path>` to load prompt from file
- Add `/prompt-save <name>` to save current prompt history to `~/.omega/prompts/<name>.md`
- Add `/prompt-template <name>` to use pre-built templates (e.g., "audit-review", "feature-spec")
- Show file size warning: `[WARN] Prompt size 245KB — recommend chunking`

**Example**:
```
you › /prompt-file /tmp/comprehensive_upgrade.md

[LOAD] Reading /tmp/comprehensive_upgrade.md (187 KB)
[WARN] Large file detected. Chunking into 3 segments...
  Segment 1: 62 KB (phases 1-2)
  Segment 2: 65 KB (phases 3-4)
  Segment 3: 60 KB (implementation + appendix)
Ready to send? (y/n):
```

### 3.3 Git Integration & Diff Preview
**Goal**: Show code diffs, branch context, and git operations from CLI.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/git status` shorthand for repo status
- Add `/git-diff [file]` to show and paginate diffs
- Add `/git-commit [msg]` to stage and commit from CLI
- Add `/push-pr [title]` to create PR with auto-generated description

**Example**:
```
you › /git-diff omega/cli/omega

[DIFF] Showing changes in OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega

@@@ -164,10 +164,12 @@@ def print_banner(session_id: str):
   def print_banner(session_id: str):
-      console.print("  [banner]██████╗[/banner]")
+      colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", ...]
+      for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):
+          color = colors[i % len(colors)]
+          console.print(f"  [{color}]{line}[/{color}]")

commit? (y/n):
```

### 3.4 Multi-Modal Input & Output
**Goal**: Support voice commands, image uploads for vision analysis, and raw code/data pasting.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/voice` mode for speech-to-text (requires microphone)
- Add `/image <path>` to upload image for vision analysis: "Describe this screenshot"
- Add `/paste` to enter multi-line mode (Ctrl+D to end)
- Add `/code <language>` to start syntax-highlighted code block input

**Example**:
```
you › /code python

[PASTE] Python mode. Type code, end with Ctrl+D:

def resume_ingest():
    db = connect(OMEGA_DB2_URL)
    sweep = db.query("...")
    for item in sweep:
        process(item)
^D

[CODE] Received 156 bytes of Python code. Analyze?
```

### 3.5 Response Streaming & Pagination
**Goal**: Stream long responses, allow pausing and resuming, rich formatting.

**Implementation**:
- Real-time streaming with pause/resume: `[pause]`, `[space] to continue`, `[q] to quit`
- Auto-paginate long responses into sections
- Syntax highlighting for code blocks with line numbers
- Table rendering with sorting: `/sort column [asc|desc]`

---

## Phase 4: Architecture & Integration

### 4.1 Event-Driven Architecture
**Goal**: CLI becomes event sink for external triggers (webhooks, scheduled tasks).

**Implementation**:
- Internal event bus: `on("ingest.progress")`, `on("db.alert")`, `on("api.error")`
- Register handlers: `/on ingest.complete → /delegate  "summarize ingest results"`
- Webhook receiver on localhost:8765 for external triggers
- Emit telemetry to ERGON.md for multi-agent awareness

### 4.2 Configuration & Theming
**Goal**: Customizable colors, key bindings, aliases, and environment variables.

**Implementation**:
- `~/.omega/cli/config.toml` for CLI settings
- `~/.omega/cli/theme.json` for color customization
- `~/.omega/cli/aliases.txt` for command shortcuts
- Built-in editor: `/config edit` opens config in $EDITOR

**Example config**:
```toml
[interface]
banner_colorshift = true
theme = "dark"
pagination_size = 50

[api]
timeout_seconds = 120
max_payload_kb = 100
retry_count = 3

[skills]
auto_suggest = true
manifest_refresh = "startup"

[monitoring]
enable_metrics = true
db_check_interval = 10
alert_threshold = 85
```

### 4.3 Plugin System
**Goal**: Allow operators to write custom CLI commands as Python plugins.

**Implementation**:
- Plugin directory: `~/.omega/cli/plugins/`
- Plugin template: `class OmegaPlugin: def register(cli) → cli.add_command(...)`
- Hot-reload: `/plugin reload <name>`
- Plugin store: `/plugin install <github-url>`

**Example plugin**:
```python
class MyPlugin(OmegaPlugin):
    def register(cli):
        @cli.command("/mycommand")
        def my_command(args):
            print("Custom plugin command!")
```

### 4.4 Audit & Compliance Logging
**Goal**: Every CLI action is logged to ERGON.md with checksums for integrity.

**Implementation**:
- Append to `/home/mega/NEXUS/ERGON.md` after each major command
- Format: `[timestamp] [CLI] <action> (exit_code: 0, duration: 2.5s, hash: abc123...)`
- Include: command, user, exit code, duration, any side effects
- Enable compliance review: `/audit --last 24h` shows all recent actions

**Example log entry**:
```
[2026-03-30 14:32] [CLI] resume_sweep2 (exit_code: 0, duration: 847s)
  → Items processed: 2,143
  → DB2 utilization: 47.3% (was 28.1%)
  → Peak memory: 512 MB
  hash: 7f4a9c2e1b8d6f3a
```

---

## Phase 5: Deployment & Documentation

### 5.1 Version Management
**Goal**: Track CLI version, auto-update, migration guides for breaking changes.

**Implementation**:
- Version file: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/VERSION` (e.g., `2.1.0`)
- Semver compliance and changelog at `CODE/CHANGELOG.md`
- `/upgrade` checks for new version and pulls latest code
- Migration guide auto-display when major version changes

### 5.2 Testing & Validation
**Goal**: CLI has comprehensive tests, validation hooks, and smoke tests.

**Implementation**:
- Unit tests: test all commands with mock API responses
- Integration tests: test real API calls against staging
- Smoke tests: `/test smoke` runs quick validation
- Coverage tracking: `/test coverage`

---

**Part 3 coming next: Detailed implementation roadmap and timeline.**

# OmegA CLI Enhancement Prompt (Part 3/4)

## Implementation Roadmap

### Sprint 1 (Week 1): Core UX Fixes
**Effort**: 16 hours | **Blocker**: None

1. **Banner Colorshifting** (1h)
   - Status: ✓ DONE (already implemented in this session)
   - File: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega` line 166

2. **Error 500 Better Diagnostics** (2h)
   - Status: ✓ DONE (already implemented in this session)
   - Detects payload size, suggests chunking
   - File: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega` line 238

3. **Smart Command Suggestions** (4h)
   - Read `~/.omega/cli/history`
   - Query skill manifest
   - Filter by relevance (fuzzy match + context)
   - Implement with `prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest`

4. **Rich Response Rendering** (4h)
   - Detect markdown, code blocks, tables, JSON
   - Use `rich.syntax.Syntax` for code
   - Use `rich.table.Table` for structured data
   - Handle large responses with pagination

5. **Session State Display** (3h)
   - Show `[tokens: X / cost: $Y]` in status
   - Add `/session stats` command
   - Persist metrics to session file

6. **Testing** (2h)
   - Unit tests for new features
   - Smoke test: `/test smoke`

### Sprint 2 (Week 2): Agentic & Skill Integration
**Effort**: 20 hours | **Blocker**: Skill manifest finalized

1. **Skill Invocation** (5h)
   - Parse `/home/mega/claude-skill-bundle/skills/skills-manifest.json`
   - Add `/skill <name> [args]` command
   - Stream skill output to CLI
   - Error handling for missing skills

2. **Agent Delegation** (5h)
   - Add `/delegate <agent> <task>` command
   - Route to agent OS
   - Stream results back
   - Save to session

3. **Workflow Chaining** (5h)
   - Parse `/chain skill1 → skill2 → skill3` syntax
   - Build dependency DAG
   - Validate before execution
   - Variable substitution: `{prev.output}`

4. **Memory Integration** (3h)
   - Connect to `OMEGA_DB2_URL`
   - Query for context embeddings
   - Show related memories on prompt
   - Append interactions to memory

5. **Testing** (2h)
   - Integration tests with agent OS
   - Mock skill responses

### Sprint 3 (Week 3): Advanced Features
**Effort**: 18 hours | **Blocker**: DB monitoring tools

1. **Real-Time Monitoring Dashboard** (6h)
   - Create `/monitor` command
   - Display DB1/DB2 utilization
   - API health, session metrics
   - Use `rich.live.Live` for updates
   - Refresh interval configurable

2. **File-Based Prompt Management** (4h)
   - `/prompt-file <path>` loader
   - `/prompt-save <name>` saver
   - `/prompt-template` library
   - File size warnings and chunking advice

3. **Git Integration** (4h)
   - `/git status`, `/git-diff`, `/git-commit`
   - `/push-pr <title>` (auto-generate description)
   - Real-time diff display with syntax highlight

4. **Multi-Modal Input** (3h)
   - `/voice` mode (optional: requires mic library)
   - `/image <path>` for vision analysis
   - `/paste` for multi-line input
   - `/code <language>` for code blocks

5. **Testing** (1h)
   - Test file I/O and parsing
   - Mock git commands

### Sprint 4 (Week 4): Architecture & Polish
**Effort**: 16 hours | **Blocker**: None

1. **Event-Driven Architecture** (4h)
   - Internal event bus
   - Handler registration: `on("event")`
   - Webhook receiver on localhost:8765
   - Telemetry to ERGON.md

2. **Configuration System** (3h)
   - `~/.omega/cli/config.toml` (TOML parser)
   - `~/.omega/cli/theme.json` (color themes)
   - `~/.omega/cli/aliases.txt` (command aliases)
   - `/config edit` for interactive editing

3. **Plugin System** (3h)
   - Plugin directory: `~/.omega/cli/plugins/`
   - Plugin interface class
   - `/plugin reload`, `/plugin install`
   - Hot-reload mechanism

4. **Audit & Compliance** (3h)
   - Append to ERGON.md after each command
   - Checksum hashing for integrity
   - `/audit` command to review history
   - Format: timestamp, action, user, exit_code, duration, hash

5. **Documentation & Testing** (3h)
   - README.md with feature guide
   - Architecture doc
   - API reference for plugins
   - Full test coverage (80%+)

---

## Development Guidelines

### File Structure
```
/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/
├── omega                        # Main CLI entry point
├── omega_cli/                   # Package directory
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── core.py                  # Core CLI logic
│   ├── commands.py              # Command handlers (@cli.command)
│   ├── renderers.py             # Rich rendering functions
│   ├── agents.py                # Agent delegation
│   ├── skills.py                # Skill invocation
│   ├── monitoring.py            # DB/API monitoring
│   ├── config.py                # Configuration management
│   ├── plugins.py               # Plugin system
│   └── audit.py                 # ERGON.md logging
├── tests/
│   ├── test_commands.py
│   ├── test_agents.py
│   ├── test_skills.py
│   ├── test_renderers.py
│   └── conftest.py
├── plugins/
│   ├── example_plugin.py
│   └── README.md
├── requirements.txt             # Dependencies
├── VERSION                      # Version file (e.g., "2.1.0")
└── CHANGELOG.md
```

### Dependencies to Add
```
rich>=13.0.0            # (already present)
prompt-toolkit>=3.0.0   # (already present)
httpx>=0.24.0           # (already present)
psycopg2-binary>=2.9    # For DB monitoring
python-dotenv>=0.19.0   # For config .env files
toml>=0.10.0            # For config.toml
pydantic>=1.9.0         # For config validation
typer>=0.6.0            # (optional) easier CLI framework
```

### Code Quality Standards
- Type hints on all functions
- Docstrings for public APIs
- Error handling with actionable messages
- Logging to both console and ERGON.md
- No hardcoded secrets (use VAULT/)

### Testing Requirements
- 80%+ coverage for new code
- Unit tests for all commands
- Integration tests with mocked API
- Smoke tests for happy paths

---

## Critical Decisions

### Decision 1: Async vs Sync
**Chosen**: Keep async (httpx.AsyncClient already in place)
- Rationale: Non-blocking I/O for streamed responses
- Alternative: Switch to sync (simpler, but slower)

### Decision 2: CLI Framework
**Chosen**: Stick with prompt_toolkit + custom commands (no typer)
- Rationale: Full control, minimal dependencies, existing patterns
- Alternative: Migrate to Typer (simpler, less flexible)

### Decision 3: Database Querying
**Chosen**: Direct psycopg2 for monitoring, no ORM
- Rationale: Simple queries, minimal overhead
- Alternative: Use SQLAlchemy (overkill for this use case)

### Decision 4: Plugin Architecture
**Chosen**: Dynamic import + class registration
- Rationale: Simple, Pythonic, hot-reload capable
- Alternative: Pkg resources, entry points (more complex)

---

## Success Criteria

✓ All existing functionality preserved (backward compatible)
✓ Colorshifting banner renders correctly on all terminals
✓ Error 500 provides actionable diagnostics
✓ Smart suggestions work without slowing prompt response
✓ Skill invocation works with all 165 skills
✓ Agent delegation routes correctly to /Codex/Aider
✓ DB monitoring refreshes every 10 seconds without lag
✓ File prompts chunk automatically above 100KB
✓ All tests pass, coverage >80%
✓ Plugin system allows custom extensions
✓ ERGON.md audit log includes all CLI actions
✓ Performance: response latency <500ms for 95th percentile

---

**Part 4 coming next: Detailed implementation notes and quick-start guide.**

"""
OmegA CLI Skills — Skill invocation and management.
"""

import json
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, List
import httpx


async def invoke_skill(skill_name: str, args: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> str:
    """
    Invoke a skill from the 165-skill manifest.

    Args:
        skill_name: Name of the skill (e.g., "npm-smoke-test-fixer")
        args: Optional list of arguments to pass to the skill

    Returns:
        Skill output as string
    """
    # Load skill manifest
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"

    if not manifest_path.exists():
        return f"ERROR: Skill manifest not found at {manifest_path}"

    try:
        manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
        skills = manifest.get("skills", {})
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERROR: Failed to load skill manifest: {e}"

    # Validate skill exists
    if skill_name not in skills:
        available = list(skills.keys())[:5]
        return f"ERROR: Skill '{skill_name}' not found. Available: {', '.join(available)}"

    skill_info = skills[skill_name]

    # For now, return skill metadata
    # In production, this would call the Agent OS via HTTP
    output = f"[SKILL] {skill_name}\n"
    output += f"  Description: {skill_info.get('description', 'No description')}\n"
    output += f"  Status: READY (invoke via Agent OS)\n"

    return output


def list_skills(pattern: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    List all available skills, optionally filtered by pattern.

    Args:
        pattern: Optional substring to filter skills

    Returns:
        List of skill dicts with name, description
    """
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"

    try:
        manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
        skills = manifest.get("skills", {})
    except:
        return []

    results = []
    for name, info in skills.items():
        if pattern and pattern.lower() not in name.lower():
            continue
        results.append({
            "name": name,
            "description": info.get("description", ""),
        })

    return results


def get_skill_info(skill_name: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Get detailed info for a specific skill."""
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"

    try:
        manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
        skills = manifest.get("skills", {})
        return skills.get(skill_name)
    except:
        return None

"""
OmegA CLI Agents — Agent delegation and routing.
"""

from typing import Dict, Optional, List, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass
class Agent:
    """Represents an available agent."""
    name: str
    description: str
    capabilities: List[str]
    status: str = "AVAILABLE"


# Available agents in the crew
AGENTS = {
    "claude": Agent(
        name="Claude",
        description="Lead Architect — Plans, reviews, logs synthesis",
        capabilities=["planning", "coordination", "review", "logging"],
    ),
    "": Agent(
        name="",
        description="Strategic Analysis — SQL, research, performance",
        capabilities=["research", "analysis", "sql", "performance"],
    ),
    "codex": Agent(
        name="Codex",
        description="Architecture & Security — Code mapping, audit, security",
        capabilities=["architecture", "security", "code_review", "mapping"],
    ),
    "aider": Agent(
        name="Aider",
        description="Surgical Implementation — File editing, refactoring, tests",
        capabilities=["implementation", "testing", "refactoring"],
    ),
}


def get_agent(agent_name: str) -> Optional[Agent]:
    """Get agent by name."""
    return AGENTS.get(agent_name.lower())


def list_agents() -> Dict[str, Agent]:
    """List all available agents."""
    return AGENTS.copy()


def recommend_agent(task_description: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """
    Recommend the best agent for a task based on keywords.

    Args:
        task_description: Description of the task

    Returns:
        Agent name (e.g., "claude", "")
    """
    task_lower = task_description.lower()

    # Simple keyword matching
    if any(kw in task_lower for kw in ["database", "sql", "query", "performance", "research"]):
        return ""
    elif any(kw in task_lower for kw in ["security", "audit", "architecture", "code", "review"]):
        return "codex"
    elif any(kw in task_lower for kw in ["implement", "refactor", "test", "fix", "edit", "file"]):
        return "aider"
    else:
        return "claude"  # Default to Claude for planning


async def delegate_task(agent_name: str, task: str) -> str:
    """
    Delegate a task to an agent.

    Args:
        agent_name: Name of agent ("claude", "", etc.)
        task: Task description

    Returns:
        Task output as string
    """
    agent = get_agent(agent_name)

    if not agent:
        available = list(AGENTS.keys())
        return f"ERROR: Agent '{agent_name}' not found. Available: {', '.join(available)}"

    # For now, return delegation info
    # In production, would call Agent OS via HTTP
    output = f"[DELEGATION] → {agent.name}\n"
    output += f"  Task: {task}\n"
    output += f"  Status: QUEUED (forward to Agent OS)\n"

    return output

"""
OmegA CLI Commands — Command handlers and workflow chaining.
"""

import re
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass
class WorkflowStep:
    """Represents a step in a workflow chain."""
    step_type: str  # "skill" or "agent"
    name: str
    args: Optional[List[str]] = None
    input_var: Optional[str] = None  # e.g., "{prev.output}"


class WorkflowParser:
    """Parse and execute workflow chains."""

    @staticmethod
    def parse_chain(chain_str: str) -> List[WorkflowStep]:
        """
        Parse a workflow chain like: skill1 → skill2 → agent:claude

        Args:
            chain_str: Chain definition string

        Returns:
            List of WorkflowStep objects
        """
        steps = []
        parts = chain_str.split("→")

        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip()
            if not part:
                continue

            # Parse: type:name [args]
            if part.startswith("agent:"):
                step_type = "agent"
                name = part.replace("agent:", "").strip()
                args = []
            elif part.startswith("skill:"):
                step_type = "skill"
                name = part.replace("skill:", "").strip()
                args = []
            else:
                # Default to skill
                step_type = "skill"
                name = part
                args = []

            steps.append(WorkflowStep(
                step_type=step_type,
                name=name,
                args=args,
            ))

        return steps

    @staticmethod
    def validate_chain(steps: List[WorkflowStep]) -> tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:
        """
        Validate a workflow chain before execution.

        Args:
            steps: List of workflow steps

        Returns:
            (is_valid, error_message)
        """
        if not steps:
            return False, "Empty chain"

        for i, step in enumerate(steps):
            if not step.name:
                return False, f"Step {i}: missing name"

            if step.step_type not in ("skill", "agent"):
                return False, f"Step {i}: invalid type '{step.step_type}'"

        return True, None


def parse_command_with_args(user_input: str) -> tuple[str, List[str]]:

    """

"""
OmegA CLI Commands — Command handlers and workflow chaining.
"""

import re
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass
class WorkflowStep:
    """Represents a step in a workflow chain."""
    step_type: str  # "skill" or "agent"
    name: str
    args: Optional[List[str]] = None
    input_var: Optional[str] = None  # e.g., "{prev.output}"


class WorkflowParser:
    """Parse and execute workflow chains."""

    @staticmethod
    def parse_chain(chain_str: str) -> List[WorkflowStep]:
        """
        Parse a workflow chain like: skill1 → skill2 → agent:claude

        Args:
            chain_str: Chain definition string

        Returns:
            List of WorkflowStep objects
        """
        steps = []
        parts = chain_str.split("→")

        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip()
            if not part:
                continue

            # Parse: type:name [args]
            if part.startswith("agent:"):
                step_type = "agent"
                name = part.replace("agent:", "").strip()
                args = []
            elif part.startswith("skill:"):
                step_type = "skill"
                name = part.replace("skill:", "").strip()
                args = []
            else:
                # Default to skill
                step_type = "skill"
                name = part
                args = []

            steps.append(WorkflowStep(
                step_type=step_type,
                name=name,
                args=args,
            ))

        return steps

    @staticmethod
    def validate_chain(steps: List[WorkflowStep]) -> tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:
        """
        Validate a workflow chain before execution.

        Args:
            steps: List of workflow steps

        Returns:
            (is_valid, error_message)
        """
        if not steps:
            return False, "Empty chain"

        for i, step in enumerate(steps):
            if not step.name:
                return False, f"Step {i}: missing name"

            if step.step_type not in ("skill", "agent"):
                return False, f"Step {i}: invalid type '{step.step_type}'"

        return True, None


def parse_command_with_args(user_input: str) -> tuple[str, List[str]]:
    """
    Parse user input into command and arguments.

    Args:
        user_input: Raw user input (e.g., "/skill npm-test-fixer --dry-run")

    Returns:
        (command, args_list)
    """
    parts = user_input.strip().split(maxsplit=1)
    command = parts[0] if parts else ""
    args = (parts[1].split() if len(parts) > 1 else [])

    return command, args


def is_workflow_chain(user_input: str) -> bool:
    """Check if input looks like a workflow chain (contains →)."""
    return "→" in user_input


def parse_memory_reference(user_input: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Extract memory query from input (e.g., "#conversation history")."""
    if user_input.startswith("#"):
        return user_input[1:].strip()
    return None

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
OmegA — Sovereign Intelligence CLI
R · W · Ϝ · Y
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession
from prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest import AutoSuggestFromHistory
from prompt_toolkit.formatted_text import HTML
from prompt_toolkit.history import FileHistory
from prompt_toolkit.key_binding import KeyBindings
from prompt_toolkit.styles import Style
from rich import box
from rich.columns import Columns
from rich.console import Console
from rich.live import Live
from rich.markdown import Markdown
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.rule import Rule
from rich.syntax import Syntax
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text
from rich.theme import Theme
from rich.tree import Tree

# ── OmegA CLI modules ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
try:
    from omega_cli import monitoring, audit, utils
except ImportError:
    monitoring = None
    audit = None
    utils = None

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENV_FILES = [
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "one-true.env",
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "gateway.env",
]
READLINE_HISTORY = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "readline_history"
SESSION_LOG      = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "history.jsonl"
SESSIONS_DIR     = Path.home() / ".omega" / "sessions"
API_BASE         = os.environ.get("OMEGA_API_URL", "https://omega-sovereign.vercel.app")

for _p in [READLINE_HISTORY.parent, SESSIONS_DIR]:
    _p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── Risk classification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

READONLY_CMDS   = {"ls","ll","la","cat","head","tail","echo","pwd","env","printenv",
                   "which","file","find","grep","rg","fd","wc","diff","stat","tree",
                   "git status","git log","git diff","git show","git branch",
                   "ps","top","df","du","free","uname","whoami","id","date","uptime"}
HIGHRISK_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b(rm|rmdir|mv|cp|chmod|chown|sudo|dd|mkfs|fdisk|parted|"
    r"git\s+push|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+rebase|git\s+force|"
    r"pip\s+install|npm\s+install|apt\s+install|apt-get)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE
)

def classify_risk(cmd: str) -> str:
    base = cmd.strip().split()[0] if cmd.strip() else ""
    if any(cmd.strip().startswith(r) for r in READONLY_CMDS):
        return "readonly"
    if HIGHRISK_PATTERNS.search(cmd):
        return "high"
    return "standard"

# ── Theme ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

THEME = Theme({
    "omega":   "bold #a855f7",
    "user":    "bold #22d4f5",
    "dim":     "dim #4a4947",
    "gold":    "bold #e8af34",
    "green":   "#6bcf7f",
    "red":     "bold #ff6b6b",
    "yellow":  "#ffd93d",
    "muted":   "#797876",
    "border":  "#2a2826",
    "banner":  "bold #22d4f5",
    "high":    "bold #ff6b6b",
    "std":     "#ffd93d",
    "low":     "#6bcf7f",
    "code":    "#22d4f5",
})

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

PROMPT_STYLE = Style.from_dict({
    "prompt":       "#22d4f5 bold",
    "prompt.arrow": "#a855f7 bold",
    "":             "#cdccca",
})

# ── Env ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def load_env():
    for path in ENV_FILES:
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            k, v = line.split("=", 1)
            os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip('"').strip("'"))

# ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def new_session_id() -> str:
    return f"Ω-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d-%H%M')}"

def session_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return SESSIONS_DIR / f"{sid}.json"

def save_session(sid: str, history: list, state: dict):
    data = {
        "session_id": sid,
        "created_at": state.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
        "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "messages": history,
        "state": state,
    }
    path = session_path(sid)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load_session(sid: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    path = session_path(sid)
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_sessions() -> list:
    sessions = []
    for p in sorted(SESSIONS_DIR.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:20]:
        try:
            d = json.loads(p.read_text())
            sessions.append(d)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return sessions

# ── Git context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def git_info() -> str:
    try:
        branch = subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True
        ).strip()
        dirty = subprocess.call(
            ["git", "diff", "--quiet"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
        ) != 0
        return f"{branch}{'*' if dirty else ''}"
    except Exception:
        return ""

# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_banner(session_id: str):
    console.print()
    # Colorshifting banner — cycles through user's terminal colors
    colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", "#f97316", "#eab308", "#10b981", "#22d4f5"]
    banner_lines = [
        "██████╗     ██╗    ██╗   ███████╗    ██╗   ██╗",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║    ██║   ██╔════╝    ╚██╗ ██╔╝",
        "██████╔╝    ██║ █╗ ██║   █████╗       ╚████╔╝ ",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║███╗██║   ██╔══╝        ╚██╔╝  ",
        "██║  ██║    ╚███╔███╔╝   ██║            ██║   ",
        "╚═╝  ╚═╝    ╚══╝╚══╝    ╚═╝            ╚═╝   ",
    ]
    for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):
        color = colors[i % len(colors)]
        console.print(f"  [{color}]{line}[/{color}]")
    console.print()

    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    mode_color = "green" if token else "muted"
    mode_label = "operator" if token else "public"
    git = git_info()
    git_str = f"  [muted]git:[/muted][dim]{git}[/dim]" if git else ""
    cwd_str = f"  [muted]cwd:[/muted][dim]{Path.cwd().name}[/dim]"

    console.print(
        f"  [gold]R · W · Ϝ · Y[/gold]   [{mode_color}]{mode_label}[/{mode_color}]"
        f"  [muted]session:[/muted][dim]{session_id}[/dim]{cwd_str}{git_str}"
    )
    console.print(
        "  [muted]/help for commands  ·  /new to clear  ·  ctrl+c to exit  ·  "
        "!cmd to run shell[/muted]"
    )
    console.print()

# ── Response state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

last_state: dict = {}

# ── Streaming chat ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def stream_response(user: str, history: list, inject_context: str = "") -> str:
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "x-omega-operator-token": token,
    }
    msg = f"{inject_context}\n\n{user}".strip() if inject_context else user
    payload = {"user": msg, "history": history, "voiceMode": False}

    full_response = ""

    with console.status("[muted]connecting…[/muted]", spinner="dots",
                        spinner_style="#a855f7") as status:
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120) as client:
                async with client.stream(
                    "POST", f"{API_BASE}/api/chat",
                    headers=headers, json=payload,
                ) as resp:
                    # Capture response headers for /state
                    global last_state
                    last_state = {
                        "mode":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Mode", "?"),
                        "provider":   resp.headers.get("X-Provider", "?"),
                        "risk":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Risk-Score", "?"),
                        "outcome":    resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Outcome", "?"),
                        "session_id": resp.headers.get("X-Session-Id", "?"),

# ── Shell command execution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def run_shell_command(cmd: str) -> bool:
    """Execute a bang-prefixed command with risk gate. Returns True if executed."""
    risk = classify_risk(cmd)

    if risk == "high":
        console.print(f"\n[high]  ⚠  HIGH RISK:[/high] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]This operation is destructive. Continue? [y/N][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer != "y":
            console.print("  [muted]  blocked.[/muted]\n")
            return False
    elif risk == "standard":
        console.print(f"\n[std]  ▸ standard:[/std] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]Execute? [Y/n][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer == "n":
            console.print("  [muted]  skipped.[/muted]\n")
            return False

    console.print()
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True,
                                capture_output=False)
        if result.returncode != 0:
            console.print(f"  [muted]exit code {result.returncode}[/muted]")
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]  shell error: {e}[/red]")
    console.print()
    return True

# ── Slash commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def cmd_help():
    console.print()
    console.print(Rule("[muted]commands[/muted]", style="#2a2826"))
    rows = [
        ("/new",           "clear conversation history"),
        ("/exit /quit",    "quit"),
        ("/state",         "show last response state (mode, provider, risk, outcome)"),
        ("/chain",         "show Phylactery info from last response"),
        ("/sessions",      "list saved sessions"),
        ("/resume [id]",   "resume a previous session"),
        ("/save",          "save current session now"),
        ("/read <path>",   "read a file and add to next message context"),
        ("/search <q>",    "grep codebase for a pattern"),
        ("/files [path]",  "browse file tree"),
        ("/dashboard",     "system status"),
        ("/history",       "show current conversation turns"),
        ("/help",          "this menu"),
        ("",               ""),
        ("!<cmd>",         "run shell command (with risk gate)"),
        ("ctrl+l",         "clear screen and reprint banner"),
        ("ctrl+r",         "search input history"),
        ("↑ / ↓",          "scroll input history"),
    ]
    t = Table(box=None, padding=(0, 2), show_header=False)
    t.add_column(style="user", no_wrap=True)
    t.add_column(style="muted")
    for cmd, desc in rows:
        t.add_row(cmd, desc)
    console.print(t)
    console.print(Rule(style="#2a2826"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_state():
    if not last_state:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no state yet — send a message first[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    t = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, border_style="#2a2826", show_header=False, padding=(0, 1))
    t.add_column(style="muted", no_wrap=True)
    t.add_column(style="dim")
    for k, v in last_state.items():
        t.add_row(k, str(v))
    console.print(Panel(t, title="[omega]state vector[/omega]",
                        border_style="#2a2826", title_align="left"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_chain():
    if not last_state:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no chain data yet[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    ph = last_state.get("phylactery", "?")
    ev = last_state.get("envelope", "?")
    console.print(Panel(
        f"[muted]phylactery head:[/muted] [dim]{ph}[/dim]\n"
        f"[muted]envelope version:[/muted] [dim]{ev}[/dim]\n"
        f"[muted]stack:[/muted] [dim]{last_state.get('stack','?')}[/dim]",
        title="[omega]Ω chain[/omega]",
        border_style="#2a2826",
        title_align="left",
    ))
    console.print()


async def cmd_sessions():
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    t = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, border_style="#2a2826",
              title="[omega]saved sessions[/omega]", title_justify="left")
    t.add_column("session id", style="user", no_wrap=True)
    t.add_column("turns", style="dim", justify="right")
    t.add_column("updated", style="muted")
    t.add_column("model", style="dim")
    for s in sessions:
        turns = len(s.get("messages", [])) // 2
        updated = s.get("updated_at", "")[:16].replace("T", " ")
        model = s.get("state", {}).get("provider", "?")
        t.add_row(s.get("session_id", "?"), str(turns), updated, model)
    console.print(t)
    console.print()
    return sessions


async def cmd_resume(args: str, current_session_id: str, history: list, state: dict):
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions to resume[/muted]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    if args:
        sid = args.strip()
    else:
        # Show picker
        await cmd_sessions()
        console.print("  [muted]enter session id to resume (or press enter to cancel):[/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            sid = input().strip()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            sid = ""
        if not sid:
            return current_session_id, history, state

    data = load_session(sid)
    if not data:
        # Try partial match
        for s in sessions:
            if sid in s.get("session_id", ""):
                data = s
                sid = s["session_id"]
                break

    if not data:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  session not found: {sid}[/red]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    loaded_history = data.get("messages", [])
    loaded_state   = data.get("state", {})
    loaded_state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat())

    console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ resumed:[/green] [dim]{sid}[/dim]  "
                  f"[muted]({len(loaded_history)//2} turns)[/muted]\n")
    # Show last 2 turns
    for msg in loaded_history[-4:]:
        role_tag = "[user]you ›[/user]" if msg["role"] == "user" else "[omega]Ω ›[/omega]"
        preview = msg["text"][:200].replace("\n", " ")
        console.print(f"  {role_tag}  [dim]{preview}…[/dim]")

584	async def cmd_files(args: str):
585	    base = Path(args.strip()).expanduser() if args.strip() else Path.cwd()
586	    if not base.exists():
587	        console.print(f"\n[red]  path not found: {base}[/red]\n")
588	        return
589	    console.print()
590	    tree = Tree(f"[code]{base.name}/[/code]", guide_style="#2a2826")
591	
592	    def add_children(node, path: Path, depth: int = 0):
593	        if depth > 3:
594	            return
595	        try:
596	            items = sorted(path.iterdir(), key=lambda x: (x.is_file(), x.name))
597	        except PermissionError:
598	            return
599	        shown = 0
600	        for item in items:
601	            if item.name.startswith(".") or item.name in (
602	                "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".git", "dist", ".next",
603	                "venv", ".venv", "target", "build"
604	            ):
605	                continue
606	            if shown >= 20:
607	                node.add("[muted]…[/muted]")
608	                break
609	            if item.is_dir():
610	                branch = node.add(f"[gold]{item.name}/[/gold]")
611	                add_children(branch, item, depth + 1)
612	            else:
613	                size = item.stat().st_size
614	                size_str = f"[muted]{size:,}b[/muted]" if size < 10000 else f"[muted]{size//1024}kb[/muted]"
615	                node.add(f"[dim]{item.name}[/dim]  {size_str}")
616	            shown += 1
617	
618	    add_children(tree, base)
619	    console.print(Panel(tree, border_style="#2a2826",
620	                        title=f"[code]{base}[/code]", title_align="left"))
621	    console.print()
