async def cmd_resume(args: str, current_session_id: str, history: list, state: dict):
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions to resume[/muted]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    if args:
        sid = args.strip()
    else:
        # Show picker
        await cmd_sessions()
        console.print("  [muted]enter session id to resume (or press enter to cancel):[/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            sid = input().strip()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            sid = ""
        if not sid:
            return current_session_id, history, state

    data = load_session(sid)
    if not data:
        # Try partial match
        for s in sessions:
            if sid in s.get("session_id", ""):
                data = s
                sid = s["session_id"]
                break

    if not data:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  session not found: {sid}[/red]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    loaded_history = data.get("messages", [])
    loaded_state   = data.get("state", {})
    loaded_state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat())

    console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ resumed:[/green] [dim]{sid}[/dim]  "
                  f"[muted]({len(loaded_history)//2} turns)[/muted]\n")
    # Show last 2 turns
    for msg in loaded_history[-4:]:
        role_tag = "[user]you ›[/user]" if msg["role"] == "user" else "[omega]Ω ›[/omega]"
        preview = msg["text"][:200].replace("\n", " ")
        console.print(f"  {role_tag}  [dim]{preview}…[/dim]")
    console.print()

    return sid, loaded_history, loaded_state


async def cmd_read(args: str) -> str:
    """Read a file and return its content as context injection."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /read <path>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    path = Path(args.strip()).expanduser()
    if not path.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  file not found: {path}[/red]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        content = path.read_text(errors="replace")
        size = len(content)
        if size > 50_000:
            content = content[:50_000] + "\n… (truncated)"
        # Detect language for syntax highlight
        suffix = path.suffix.lstrip(".")
        lang_map = {"py": "python", "ts": "typescript", "tsx": "tsx",
                    "js": "javascript", "rs": "rust", "go": "go",
                    "json": "json", "md": "markdown", "sh": "bash",
                    "toml": "toml", "yaml": "yaml", "yml": "yaml",
                    "css": "css", "html": "html", "sql": "sql"}
        lang = lang_map.get(suffix, "text")
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            Syntax(content[:3000], lang, theme="monokai",
                   line_numbers=True, word_wrap=True)
            if lang != "text" else Text(content[:3000], style="dim"),
            title=f"[code]{path.name}[/code]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
            subtitle=f"[muted]{size:,} chars — injecting into next message[/muted]",
        ))
        console.print()
        return f"[File: {path}]\n```{lang}\n{content}\n```"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  read error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_search(args: str) -> str:
    """Grep the codebase and optionally inject results."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /search <pattern>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             "--include=*.go", "--include=*.md",
             "-l", args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        files = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
        if not files:
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  no matches for:[/muted] {args}\n")
            return ""

        # Show matches
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            "\n".join(f"[code]{f}[/code]" for f in files[:30]),
            title=f"[omega]search: {args}[/omega]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
        ))
        console.print()

        # Get actual line matches (first 20)
        result2 = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        matches = result2.stdout.strip()[:3000]
        return f"[Search results for '{args}']\n{matches}"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  search error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_files(args: str):
    base = Path(args.strip()).expanduser() if args.strip() else Path.cwd()
    if not base.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  path not found: {base}[/red]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    tree = Tree(f"[code]{base.name}/[/code]", guide_style="#2a2826")

    def add_children(node, path: Path, depth: int = 0):
        if depth > 3:
            return
        try:
            items = sorted(path.iterdir(), key=lambda x: (x.is_file(), x.name))
        except PermissionError:
            return
        shown = 0
        for item in items:
            if item.name.startswith(".") or item.name in (
                "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".git", "dist", ".next",
                "venv", ".venv", "target", "build"
            ):
                continue
            if shown >= 20:
                node.add("[muted]…[/muted]")
                break
            if item.is_dir():
                branch = node.add(f"[gold]{item.name}/[/gold]")
                add_children(branch, item, depth + 1)
            else:
                size = item.stat().st_size
                size_str = f"[muted]{size:,}b[/muted]" if size < 10000 else f"[muted]{size//1024}kb[/muted]"
                node.add(f"[dim]{item.name}[/dim]  {size_str}")
            shown += 1

    add_children(tree, base)
    console.print(Panel(tree, border_style="#2a2826",
                        title=f"[code]{base}[/code]", title_align="left"))
    console.print()

# ── Key bindings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def make_bindings() -> KeyBindings:
    kb = KeyBindings()

    @kb.add("c-l")
    def _clear(event):
        event.app.renderer.reset()
        os.system("clear")

    return kb

# ── REPL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def repl(resume_id: Optional[str] = None):
    session: PromptSession = PromptSession(
        history=FileHistory(str(READLINE_HISTORY)),
        auto_suggest=AutoSuggestFromHistory(),
        style=PROMPT_STYLE,
        key_bindings=make_bindings(),
        enable_history_search=True,
        mouse_support=False,
    )

    session_id = resume_id or new_session_id()
    history: list = []
    state: dict   = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}

    # Resume if requested
    if resume_id:
        data = load_session(resume_id)
        if data:
            history = data.get("messages", [])
            state   = data.get("state", state)
            state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", state["created_at"])

    pending_context = ""   # injected by /read or /search
    save_counter    = 0

    print_banner(session_id)

    while True:
        try:
            line = await session.prompt_async(
                HTML("<prompt>you</prompt> <prompt.arrow>›</prompt.arrow> ")
            )
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved → {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue

        # ── Bang commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if line.startswith("!"):
            await run_shell_command(line[1:].strip())
            continue

        # ── Slash commands ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        parts = line.split(None, 1)
        cmd   = parts[0].lower()
        args  = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""

        if cmd in ("/exit", "/quit", "exit", "quit"):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved → {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        if cmd == "/new":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            history.clear()
            pending_context = ""
            session_id = new_session_id()
            state = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  new session:[/muted] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/help":
            await cmd_help()
            continue

        if cmd == "/state":
            await cmd_state()
            continue

        if cmd == "/chain":
            await cmd_chain()
            continue

        if cmd == "/sessions":
            await cmd_sessions()
            continue

        if cmd == "/resume":
            session_id, history, state = await cmd_resume(
                args, session_id, history, state
            )
            continue

        if cmd == "/save":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ saved:[/green] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/read":
            pending_context = await cmd_read(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]file loaded — it will be injected into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/search":
            pending_context = await cmd_search(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]results loaded — injecting into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/files":
            await cmd_files(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/dashboard":
            await cmd_dashboard(session_id, history)
            continue

        if cmd in ("/history", "/log"):
            await cmd_history_show(history)
            continue

        # ── /monitor command ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd.startswith("/monitor"):
            if monitoring:
                console.print("\n[cyan]Starting real-time monitoring (Ctrl+C to stop)...[/cyan]\n")
                db1_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB_URL", "")
                db2_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB2_URL", "")
                try:
                    monitoring.monitor_live(db1_url, db2_url, API_BASE, interval=10)
                except Exception as e:
                    console.print(f"\n[red]Error during monitoring: {e}[/red]\n")
                # Log to ERGON
                if audit:
                    audit.log_cli_action("/monitor", exit_code=0, duration_sec=0)
            else:
                console.print("\n[red]Monitoring module not available[/red]\n")
            continue

        # ── /help command ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd in ("/help", "/h", "/?"):
            console.print("\n[cyan]Available commands:[/cyan]")
            console.print("  /monitor              Show real-time DB and API status")
            console.print("  /new                  Start a new session")
            console.print("  /sessions             List saved sessions")
            console.print("  /state                Show last response state")
            console.print("  !cmd                  Run shell command")
            console.print("  Ctrl+C                Exit\n")
            continue

        # ── Unknown slash command ───────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd.startswith("/"):
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  unknown command: {cmd}  (try /help)[/muted]\n")
            continue

        # ── Send to OmegA ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        inject  = pending_context
        pending_context = ""

        reply = await stream_response(line, history, inject_context=inject)

        if reply:
            history.append({"role": "user",  "text": line})
            history.append({"role": "omega", "text": reply})
            state["provider"] = last_state.get("provider", "?")

            # Auto-save every 10 turns
            save_counter += 1
            if save_counter % 10 == 0:
                save_session(session_id, history, state)

# ── One-shot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def one_shot(prompt: str) -> int:
    reply = await stream_response(prompt, [])
    return 0 if reply else 1

# ── Entry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    load_env()
    args = sys.argv[1:]

    if not args:
        if not sys.stdin.isatty():
            prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
            if prompt:
                return asyncio.run(one_shot(prompt))
        asyncio.run(repl())
        return 0

    if args[0] in ("-h", "--help", "help"):
        console.print("[user]omega[/user]                    interactive REPL")
        console.print('[user]omega "question"[/user]         one-shot query')
        console.print("[user]omega resume[/user]             pick a session to resume")
        console.print("[user]omega resume Ω-2026-...[/user]  resume specific session")
        return 0

    if args[0] == "resume":
        sid = args[1] if len(args) > 1 else None
        asyncio.run(repl(resume_id=sid))
        return 0

    return asyncio.run(one_shot(" ".join(args)))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        sys.exit(main())
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        console.print("\n[muted]  goodbye.[/muted]\n")
        sys.exit(0)

"""
OmegA CLI Prompts — File-based prompt management and templates.
"""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any


class PromptManager:
    """Manages file-based prompts and templates."""

    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize prompt manager."""
        self.prompts_dir = Path.home() / ".omega" / "prompts"
        self.templates_dir = self.prompts_dir / "templates"

        # Create directories
        self.prompts_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self.templates_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Create default templates
        self._init_templates()

    def _init_templates(self) -> None:
        """Initialize default prompt templates."""
        templates = {
            "audit-review": """# Code Audit Review

## Files to Review
[Paste file paths or code here]

## Focus Areas
- Code quality and style
- Security issues
- Performance concerns
- Best practices violations

## Questions
1. What are the main issues found?
2. What should be prioritized?
3. Are there patterns or anti-patterns?
""",
            "feature-spec": """# Feature Specification

## Summary
[Brief description of the feature]

## Requirements
- [ ] Requirement 1
- [ ] Requirement 2
- [ ] Requirement 3

## Implementation Notes
[Any special considerations]

## Testing
[How to verify this works]
""",
            "bug-report": """# Bug Report

## Description
[What's the issue?]

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Step 1
2. Step 2
3. Step 3

## Expected Behavior
[What should happen]

## Actual Behavior
[What actually happens]

## Environment
- OS:
- Version:
- [Other relevant info]
""",
        }

        for name, content in templates.items():
            template_file = self.templates_dir / f"{name}.md"
            if not template_file.exists():
                template_file.write_text(content)

    def load_prompt(self, path: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Load a prompt from a file."""
        file_path = Path(path).expanduser()

        if not file_path.exists():
            return None

        try:
            return file_path.read_text()
        except Exception:
            return None

    def save_prompt(self, name: str, content: str) -> bool:
        """Save a prompt to file."""
        try:
            prompt_file = self.prompts_dir / f"{name}.md"
            prompt_file.write_text(content)
            return True
        except Exception:
            return False

    def list_saved_prompts(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all saved prompts."""
        prompts = [f.stem for f in self.prompts_dir.glob("*.md")]
        return sorted(prompts)

    def list_templates(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all available templates."""
        templates = [f.stem for f in self.templates_dir.glob("*.md")]
        return sorted(templates)

    def get_template(self, name: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Get a template by name."""
        template_file = self.templates_dir / f"{name}.md"

        if template_file.exists():
            try:
                return template_file.read_text()
            except Exception:
                return None

        return None

    def chunk_prompt(self, content: str, chunk_size_kb: int = 100) -> List[str]:
        """Chunk a large prompt into smaller segments."""
        chunk_size_bytes = chunk_size_kb * 1024
        chunks = []

        # Split by double newlines first (paragraphs)
        paragraphs = content.split("\n\n")
        current_chunk = ""

        for para in paragraphs:
            if len((current_chunk + para).encode()) > chunk_size_bytes:
                if current_chunk:
                    chunks.append(current_chunk.strip())
                current_chunk = para
            else:
                current_chunk += f"\n\n{para}" if current_chunk else para

        if current_chunk:
            chunks.append(current_chunk.strip())

        return chunks


# Global instance
_prompt_manager: Optional[PromptManager] = None


def get_prompt_manager() -> PromptManager:
    """Get the global prompt manager instance."""
    global _prompt_manager
    if _prompt_manager is None:
        _prompt_manager = PromptManager()
    return _prompt_manager

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
OmegA — Sovereign Intelligence CLI
R · W · Ϝ · Y
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession
from prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest import AutoSuggestFromHistory
from prompt_toolkit.formatted_text import HTML
from prompt_toolkit.history import FileHistory
from prompt_toolkit.key_binding import KeyBindings
from prompt_toolkit.styles import Style
from rich import box
from rich.columns import Columns
from rich.console import Console
from rich.live import Live
from rich.markdown import Markdown
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.rule import Rule
from rich.syntax import Syntax
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text
from rich.theme import Theme
from rich.tree import Tree

# ── OmegA CLI modules ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
try:
    from omega_cli import monitoring, audit, utils, skills, agents, commands, config, plugins
except ImportError:
    monitoring = None
    audit = None
    utils = None
    skills = None
    agents = None
    commands = None
    config = None
    plugins = None

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENV_FILES = [
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "one-true.env",
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "gateway.env",
]
READLINE_HISTORY = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "readline_history"
SESSION_LOG      = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "history.jsonl"
SESSIONS_DIR     = Path.home() / ".omega" / "sessions"
API_BASE         = os.environ.get("OMEGA_API_URL", "https://omega-sovereign.vercel.app")

for _p in [READLINE_HISTORY.parent, SESSIONS_DIR]:
    _p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── Risk classification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

READONLY_CMDS   = {"ls","ll","la","cat","head","tail","echo","pwd","env","printenv",
                   "which","file","find","grep","rg","fd","wc","diff","stat","tree",
                   "git status","git log","git diff","git show","git branch",
                   "ps","top","df","du","free","uname","whoami","id","date","uptime"}
HIGHRISK_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b(rm|rmdir|mv|cp|chmod|chown|sudo|dd|mkfs|fdisk|parted|"
    r"git\s+push|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+rebase|git\s+force|"
    r"pip\s+install|npm\s+install|apt\s+install|apt-get)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE
)

def classify_risk(cmd: str) -> str:
    base = cmd.strip().split()[0] if cmd.strip() else ""
    if any(cmd.strip().startswith(r) for r in READONLY_CMDS):
        return "readonly"
    if HIGHRISK_PATTERNS.search(cmd):
        return "high"
    return "standard"

# ── Theme ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

THEME = Theme({
    "omega":   "bold #a855f7",
    "user":    "bold #22d4f5",
    "dim":     "dim #4a4947",
    "gold":    "bold #e8af34",
    "green":   "#6bcf7f",
    "red":     "bold #ff6b6b",
    "yellow":  "#ffd93d",
    "muted":   "#797876",
    "border":  "#2a2826",
    "banner":  "bold #22d4f5",
    "high":    "bold #ff6b6b",
    "std":     "#ffd93d",
    "low":     "#6bcf7f",
    "code":    "#22d4f5",
})

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

PROMPT_STYLE = Style.from_dict({
    "prompt":       "#22d4f5 bold",
    "prompt.arrow": "#a855f7 bold",
    "":             "#cdccca",
})

# ── Env ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def load_env():
    for path in ENV_FILES:
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            k, v = line.split("=", 1)
            os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip('"').strip("'"))

# ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def new_session_id() -> str:
    return f"Ω-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d-%H%M')}"

def session_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return SESSIONS_DIR / f"{sid}.json"

def save_session(sid: str, history: list, state: dict):
    data = {
        "session_id": sid,
        "created_at": state.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
        "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "messages": history,
        "state": state,
    }
    path = session_path(sid)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load_session(sid: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    path = session_path(sid)
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_sessions() -> list:
    sessions = []
    for p in sorted(SESSIONS_DIR.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:20]:
        try:
            d = json.loads(p.read_text())
            sessions.append(d)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return sessions

# ── Git context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def git_info() -> str:
    try:
        branch = subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True
        ).strip()
        dirty = subprocess.call(
            ["git", "diff", "--quiet"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
        ) != 0
        return f"{branch}{'*' if dirty else ''}"
    except Exception:
        return ""

# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_banner(session_id: str):
    console.print()
    # Colorshifting banner — cycles through user's terminal colors
    colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", "#f97316", "#eab308", "#10b981", "#22d4f5"]
    banner_lines = [
        "██████╗     ██╗    ██╗   ███████╗    ██╗   ██╗",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║    ██║   ██╔════╝    ╚██╗ ██╔╝",
        "██████╔╝    ██║ █╗ ██║   █████╗       ╚████╔╝ ",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║███╗██║   ██╔══╝        ╚██╔╝  ",
        "██║  ██║    ╚███╔███╔╝   ██║            ██║   ",
        "╚═╝  ╚═╝    ╚══╝╚══╝    ╚═╝            ╚═╝   ",
    ]
    for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):
        color = colors[i % len(colors)]
        console.print(f"  [{color}]{line}[/{color}]")
    console.print()

    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    mode_color = "green" if token else "muted"
    mode_label = "operator" if token else "public"
    git = git_info()
    git_str = f"  [muted]git:[/muted][dim]{git}[/dim]" if git else ""
    cwd_str = f"  [muted]cwd:[/muted][dim]{Path.cwd().name}[/dim]"

    console.print(
        f"  [gold]R · W · Ϝ · Y[/gold]   [{mode_color}]{mode_label}[/{mode_color}]"
        f"  [muted]session:[/muted][dim]{session_id}[/dim]{cwd_str}{git_str}"
    )
    console.print(
        "  [muted]/help for commands  ·  /new to clear  ·  ctrl+c to exit  ·  "
        "!cmd to run shell[/muted]"
    )
    console.print()

# ── Response state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

last_state: dict = {}

# ── Streaming chat ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def stream_response(user: str, history: list, inject_context: str = "") -> str:
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "x-omega-operator-token": token,
    }
    msg = f"{inject_context}\n\n{user}".strip() if inject_context else user
    payload = {"user": msg, "history": history, "voiceMode": False}

    full_response = ""

    with console.status("[muted]connecting…[/muted]", spinner="dots",
                        spinner_style="#a855f7") as status:
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120) as client:
                async with client.stream(
                    "POST", f"{API_BASE}/api/chat",
                    headers=headers, json=payload,
                ) as resp:
                    # Capture response headers for /state
                    global last_state
                    last_state = {
                        "mode":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Mode", "?"),
                        "provider":   resp.headers.get("X-Provider", "?"),
                        "risk":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Risk-Score", "?"),
                        "outcome":    resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Outcome", "?"),
                        "session_id": resp.headers.get("X-Session-Id", "?"),
                        "envelope":   resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Envelope-Version", "?"),
                        "phylactery": resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Phylactery-Head", "?"),
                        "stack":      resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Stack", "?"),
                    }

                    if resp.status_code != 200:
                        body = await resp.aread()
                        body_text = body.decode()
                        status.stop()

                        # Better error diagnostics
                        if resp.status_code == 500:
                            console.print(f"\n[red]  error 500: internal server error[/red]")
                            payload_size = len(json.dumps(payload))
                            if payload_size > 100000:  # ~100KB
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  hint: request size ~{payload_size//1024}KB (too large)[/yellow]")
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  try: split your prompt into smaller pieces[/yellow]")
                            else:
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  hint: {body_text[:200]}[/yellow]")
                        else:
                            console.print(f"\n[red]  error {resp.status_code}: {body_text[:200]}[/red]\n")
                        return ""

                    status.update("[muted]synthesizing…[/muted]")

                    buf = ""
                    async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                        buf += chunk
                        while "\n" in buf:
                            line, buf = buf.split("\n", 1)
                            if line.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                                continue
                            if line.strip():
                                full_response += line + "\n"
                    if buf.strip() and not buf.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                        full_response += buf

        except httpx.ConnectError:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  connection error — {API_BASE} unreachable[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except httpx.TimeoutException:
            status.stop()
            console.print("\n[red]  request timed out[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except Exception as e:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  error: {e}[/red]\n")
            return ""

    if not full_response.strip():
        return ""

    _render_response(full_response.strip(), history=history)
    return full_response.strip()


def _render_response(text: str, history: list = None):
    console.print()
    is_markdown = any(m in text for m in ("**", "##", "```", "\n- ", "\n* ", "\n1. ", "> "))
    if is_markdown:
        console.print(Panel(
            Markdown(text),
            border_style="#2a2826",
            padding=(0, 2),
            title="[omega]Ω[/omega]",
            title_align="left",
        ))
    else:
        # Wrap long plain text
        console.print(f"[omega]Ω ›[/omega]  {text}")
    console.print()

    # Show smart suggestions (if available and history provided)
    if utils and history:
        try:
            suggestions = utils.get_smart_suggestions(text, history)
            if suggestions:
                console.print("[dim]Suggestions:[/dim]")
                for i, s in enumerate(suggestions, 1):
                    console.print(
                        f"  {i}. [{s['type']}] {s['label']:<30} "
                        f"[dim]— {s['description']}[/dim]"
                    )
                console.print()
        except Exception:
            pass  # Suggestions are optional

# ── Shell command execution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def run_shell_command(cmd: str) -> bool:
    """Execute a bang-prefixed command with risk gate. Returns True if executed."""
    risk = classify_risk(cmd)

    if risk == "high":
        console.print(f"\n[high]  ⚠  HIGH RISK:[/high] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]This operation is destructive. Continue? [y/N][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer != "y":
            console.print("  [muted]  blocked.[/muted]\n")
            return False
    elif risk == "standard":
        console.print(f"\n[std]  ▸ standard:[/std] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]Execute? [Y/n][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer == "n":
            console.print("  [muted]  skipped.[/muted]\n")
            return False

    console.print()
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True,
                                capture_output=False)
        if result.returncode != 0:
            console.print(f"  [muted]exit code {result.returncode}[/muted]")
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]  shell error: {e}[/red]")
    console.print()
    return True

async def cmd_resume(args: str, current_session_id: str, history: list, state: dict):
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions to resume[/muted]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    if args:
        sid = args.strip()
    else:
        # Show picker
        await cmd_sessions()
        console.print("  [muted]enter session id to resume (or press enter to cancel):[/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            sid = input().strip()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            sid = ""
        if not sid:
            return current_session_id, history, state

    data = load_session(sid)
    if not data:
        # Try partial match
        for s in sessions:
            if sid in s.get("session_id", ""):
                data = s
                sid = s["session_id"]
                break

    if not data:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  session not found: {sid}[/red]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    loaded_history = data.get("messages", [])
    loaded_state   = data.get("state", {})
    loaded_state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat())

    console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ resumed:[/green] [dim]{sid}[/dim]  "
                  f"[muted]({len(loaded_history)//2} turns)[/muted]\n")
    # Show last 2 turns
    for msg in loaded_history[-4:]:
        role_tag = "[user]you ›[/user]" if msg["role"] == "user" else "[omega]Ω ›[/omega]"
        preview = msg["text"][:200].replace("\n", " ")
        console.print(f"  {role_tag}  [dim]{preview}…[/dim]")
    console.print()

    return sid, loaded_history, loaded_state


async def cmd_read(args: str) -> str:
    """Read a file and return its content as context injection."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /read <path>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    path = Path(args.strip()).expanduser()
    if not path.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  file not found: {path}[/red]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        content = path.read_text(errors="replace")
        size = len(content)
        if size > 50_000:
            content = content[:50_000] + "\n… (truncated)"
        # Detect language for syntax highlight
        suffix = path.suffix.lstrip(".")
        lang_map = {"py": "python", "ts": "typescript", "tsx": "tsx",
                    "js": "javascript", "rs": "rust", "go": "go",
                    "json": "json", "md": "markdown", "sh": "bash",
                    "toml": "toml", "yaml": "yaml", "yml": "yaml",
                    "css": "css", "html": "html", "sql": "sql"}
        lang = lang_map.get(suffix, "text")
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            Syntax(content[:3000], lang, theme="monokai",
                   line_numbers=True, word_wrap=True)
            if lang != "text" else Text(content[:3000], style="dim"),
            title=f"[code]{path.name}[/code]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
            subtitle=f"[muted]{size:,} chars — injecting into next message[/muted]",
        ))
        console.print()
        return f"[File: {path}]\n```{lang}\n{content}\n```"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  read error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_search(args: str) -> str:
    """Grep the codebase and optionally inject results."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /search <pattern>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             "--include=*.go", "--include=*.md",
             "-l", args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        files = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
        if not files:
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  no matches for:[/muted] {args}\n")
            return ""

        # Show matches
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            "\n".join(f"[code]{f}[/code]" for f in files[:30]),
            title=f"[omega]search: {args}[/omega]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
        ))
        console.print()

        # Get actual line matches (first 20)
        result2 = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        matches = result2.stdout.strip()[:3000]
        return f"[Search results for '{args}']\n{matches}"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  search error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_files(args: str):
    base = Path(args.strip()).expanduser() if args.strip() else Path.cwd()
    if not base.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  path not found: {base}[/red]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    tree = Tree(f"[code]{base.name}/[/code]", guide_style="#2a2826")

    def add_children(node, path: Path, depth: int = 0):
        if depth > 3:
            return
        try:
            items = sorted(path.iterdir(), key=lambda x: (x.is_file(), x.name))
        except PermissionError:
            return
        shown = 0
        for item in items:
            if item.name.startswith(".") or item.name in (
                "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".git", "dist", ".next",
                "venv", ".venv", "target", "build"
            ):
                continue
            if shown >= 20:
                node.add("[muted]…[/muted]")
                break
            if item.is_dir():
                branch = node.add(f"[gold]{item.name}/[/gold]")
                add_children(branch, item, depth + 1)
            else:
                size = item.stat().st_size
                size_str = f"[muted]{size:,}b[/muted]" if size < 10000 else f"[muted]{size//1024}kb[/muted]"
                node.add(f"[dim]{item.name}[/dim]  {size_str}")
            shown += 1

    add_children(tree, base)
    console.print(Panel(tree, border_style="#2a2826",
                        title=f"[code]{base}[/code]", title_align="left"))
    console.print()

# ── Key bindings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def make_bindings() -> KeyBindings:
    kb = KeyBindings()

    @kb.add("c-l")
    def _clear(event):
        event.app.renderer.reset()
        os.system("clear")

    return kb

# ── REPL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def repl(resume_id: Optional[str] = None):
    session: PromptSession = PromptSession(
        history=FileHistory(str(READLINE_HISTORY)),
        auto_suggest=AutoSuggestFromHistory(),
        style=PROMPT_STYLE,
        key_bindings=make_bindings(),
        enable_history_search=True,
        mouse_support=False,
    )

    session_id = resume_id or new_session_id()
    history: list = []
    state: dict   = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}

    # Resume if requested
    if resume_id:
        data = load_session(resume_id)
        if data:
            history = data.get("messages", [])
            state   = data.get("state", state)
            state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", state["created_at"])

    pending_context = ""   # injected by /read or /search
    save_counter    = 0

    print_banner(session_id)

    while True:
        try:
            line = await session.prompt_async(
                HTML("<prompt>you</prompt> <prompt.arrow>›</prompt.arrow> ")
            )
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved → {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue

        # ── Bang commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if line.startswith("!"):
            await run_shell_command(line[1:].strip())
            continue

        # ── Slash commands ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        parts = line.split(None, 1)
        cmd   = parts[0].lower()
        args  = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""

        if cmd in ("/exit", "/quit", "exit", "quit"):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved → {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        if cmd == "/new":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            history.clear()
            pending_context = ""
            session_id = new_session_id()
            state = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  new session:[/muted] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/help":
            await cmd_help()
            continue

        if cmd == "/state":
            await cmd_state()
            continue

        if cmd == "/chain":
            await cmd_chain()
            continue

        if cmd == "/sessions":
            await cmd_sessions()
            continue

        if cmd == "/resume":
            session_id, history, state = await cmd_resume(
                args, session_id, history, state
            )
            continue

        if cmd == "/save":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[green]  ✓ saved:[/green] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/read":
            pending_context = await cmd_read(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]file loaded — it will be injected into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/search":
            pending_context = await cmd_search(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]results loaded — injecting into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/files":
            await cmd_files(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/dashboard":
            await cmd_dashboard(session_id, history)
            continue

        if cmd in ("/history", "/log"):
            await cmd_history_show(history)
            continue

        # ── /monitor command ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd.startswith("/monitor"):
            if monitoring:
                console.print("\n[cyan]Starting real-time monitoring (Ctrl+C to stop)...[/cyan]\n")
                db1_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB_URL", "")
                db2_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB2_URL", "")
                try:
                    monitoring.monitor_live(db1_url, db2_url, API_BASE, interval=10)
                except Exception as e:
                    console.print(f"\n[red]Error during monitoring: {e}[/red]\n")
                # Log to ERGON
                if audit:
                    audit.log_cli_action("/monitor", exit_code=0, duration_sec=0)
            else:
                console.print("\n[red]Monitoring module not available[/red]\n")
            continue

        # ── New Phase 2+ commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd == "/skill":
            await cmd_skill(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/delegate":
            await cmd_delegate(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/chain":
            await cmd_chain(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/config":
            await cmd_config(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/plugin":
            await cmd_plugin(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/alias":
            await cmd_alias(args)
            continue

        # ── /help command ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd in ("/help", "/h", "/?"):
            console.print("\n[cyan]Available commands:[/cyan]")
            console.print("  Session & History:")
            console.print("    /new                    Start a new session")
            console.print("    /sessions               List saved sessions")
            console.print("    /resume [id]            Resume a session")
            console.print("    /save                   Save current session")
            console.print("    /history                Show conversation history")
            console.print()
            console.print("  OmegA Features:")
            console.print("    /monitor                Show real-time DB and API status")
            console.print("    /skill <name> [args]    Invoke a skill from the manifest")
            console.print("    /delegate <agent> <task> Delegate task to an agent")
            console.print("    /chain skill1 → skill2  Build and validate workflow")
            console.print()
            console.print("  Configuration:")
            console.print("    /config [get|set]       Manage CLI settings")
            console.print("    /alias [name] [cmd]     Create command aliases")
            console.print("    /plugin [list|load]     Manage plugins")
            console.print()
            console.print("  System:")
            console.print("    /state                  Show last response metadata")
            console.print("    /read <path>            Load file for next prompt")
            console.print("    /search <pattern>       Grep codebase")
            console.print("    /files [path]           Browse file tree")
            console.print("    /dashboard              Show system info")
            console.print("    !cmd                    Run shell command")
            console.print("    Ctrl+C                  Exit\n")
            continue

        # ── Unknown slash command ───────────────────────────────────────────────
        if cmd.startswith("/"):
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  unknown command: {cmd}  (try /help)[/muted]\n")
            continue

        # ── Send to OmegA ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        inject  = pending_context
        pending_context = ""

        reply = await stream_response(line, history, inject_context=inject)

        if reply:
            history.append({"role": "user",  "text": line})
            history.append({"role": "omega", "text": reply})
            state["provider"] = last_state.get("provider", "?")

            # Auto-save every 10 turns
            save_counter += 1
            if save_counter % 10 == 0:
                save_session(session_id, history, state)

# ── One-shot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def one_shot(prompt: str) -> int:
    reply = await stream_response(prompt, [])
    return 0 if reply else 1

# ── Entry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    load_env()
    args = sys.argv[1:]

    if not args:
        if not sys.stdin.isatty():
            prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
            if prompt:
                return asyncio.run(one_shot(prompt))
        asyncio.run(repl())
        return 0

    if args[0] in ("-h", "--help", "help"):
        console.print("[user]omega[/user]                    interactive REPL")
        console.print('[user]omega "question"[/user]         one-shot query')
        console.print("[user]omega resume[/user]             pick a session to resume")
        console.print("[user]omega resume Ω-2026-...[/user]  resume specific session")
        return 0

    if args[0] == "resume":
        sid = args[1] if len(args) > 1 else None
        asyncio.run(repl(resume_id=sid))
        return 0

    return asyncio.run(one_shot(" ".join(args)))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        sys.exit(main())
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        console.print("\n[muted]  goodbye.[/muted]\n")
        sys.exit(0)

async def cmd_alias(args: str):
    """Manage command aliases."""
    if not args:
        if config:
            cfg = config.get_config()
            aliases = cfg.get_aliases()
            if aliases:
                console.print("\n[cyan]Command aliases:[/cyan]\n")
                for alias, command in aliases.items():
                    console.print(f"  {alias:15} → {command}")
                console.print()
            else:
                console.print("\n[muted]  no aliases defined[/muted]\n")
        return

    parts = args.split(None, 1)
    alias_name = parts[0]
    command = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""

    if config:
        cfg = config.get_config()
        cfg.add_alias(alias_name, command)
        console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Added alias: {alias_name} → {command}[/green]\n")
    else:
        console.print("\n[red]Config module not available[/red]\n")


async def cmd_prompt_file(args: str):
    """Load a prompt from a file."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /prompt-file <path>[/muted]\n")
        return ""

    if prompts:
        pm = prompts.get_prompt_manager()
        content = pm.load_prompt(args.strip())

        if not content:
            console.print(f"\n[red]Error: File not found or unreadable: {args}[/red]\n")
            return ""

        size_kb = len(content.encode()) / 1024
        console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Loaded {args}[/green] ({size_kb:.1f} KB)\n")

        # Warn if large
        if size_kb > 100:
            chunks = pm.chunk_prompt(content)
            console.print(f"[yellow]⚠ Large file. Consider chunking into {len(chunks)} parts.[/yellow]\n")

        return content
    else:
        console.print("\n[red]Prompts module not available[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_prompt_save(args: str):
    """Save current prompt/context to file."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /prompt-save <name>[/muted]\n")
        return

    if prompts:
        pm = prompts.get_prompt_manager()
        # Save history as prompt
        history_text = "\n\n".join([
            f"User: {msg['text']}" if msg['role'] == 'user' else f"OmegA: {msg['text']}"
            for msg in history_list
        ])

        success = pm.save_prompt(args.strip(), history_text)
        if success:
            console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Saved prompt: {args}[/green]\n")
        else:
            console.print(f"\n[red]Error saving prompt[/red]\n")
    else:
        console.print("\n[red]Prompts module not available[/red]\n")

        console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Code block ({language})[/green]\n")
        return formatted
    else:
        console.print("\n[red]Multimodal module not available[/red]\n")
        return ""

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
OmegA — Sovereign Intelligence CLI
R · W · Ϝ · Y
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession

# ── OmegA CLI modules ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
try:
    from omega_cli import monitoring, audit, utils, skills, agents, commands, config, plugins, prompts, git, multimodal
except ImportError:
    monitoring = None
    audit = None
    utils = None
    skills = None
    agents = None
    commands = None
    config = None
    plugins = None
    prompts = None
    git = None
    multimodal = None

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENV_FILES = [
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "one-true.env",
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "gateway.env",
]
READLINE_HISTORY = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "readline_history"
SESSION_LOG      = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "history.jsonl"
SESSIONS_DIR     = Path.home() / ".omega" / "sessions"
API_BASE         = os.environ.get("OMEGA_API_URL", "https://omega-sovereign.vercel.app")

for _p in [READLINE_HISTORY.parent, SESSIONS_DIR]:
    _p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── Risk classification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

READONLY_CMDS   = {"ls","ll","la","cat","head","tail","echo","pwd","env","printenv",
                   "which","file","find","grep","rg","fd","wc","diff","stat","tree",
                   "git status","git log","git diff","git show","git branch",
                   "ps","top","df","du","free","uname","whoami","id","date","uptime"}
HIGHRISK_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b(rm|rmdir|mv|cp|chmod|chown|sudo|dd|mkfs|fdisk|parted|"
    r"git\s+push|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+rebase|git\s+force|"
    r"pip\s+install|npm\s+install|apt\s+install|apt-get)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE
)

def classify_risk(cmd: str) -> str:
    base = cmd.strip().split()[0] if cmd.strip() else ""
    if any(cmd.strip().startswith(r) for r in READONLY_CMDS):
        return "readonly"
    if HIGHRISK_PATTERNS.search(cmd):
        return "high"
    return "standard"

# ── Theme ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

THEME = Theme({
    "omega":   "bold #a855f7",
    "user":    "bold #22d4f5",
    "dim":     "dim #4a4947",
    "gold":    "bold #e8af34",
    "green":   "#6bcf7f",
    "red":     "bold #ff6b6b",
    "yellow":  "#ffd93d",
    "muted":   "#797876",
    "border":  "#2a2826",
    "banner":  "bold #22d4f5",
    "high":    "bold #ff6b6b",
    "std":     "#ffd93d",
    "low":     "#6bcf7f",
    "code":    "#22d4f5",
})

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

PROMPT_STYLE = Style.from_dict({
    "prompt":       "#22d4f5 bold",
    "prompt.arrow": "#a855f7 bold",
    "":             "#cdccca",
})

# ── Env ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def load_env():
    for path in ENV_FILES:
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            k, v = line.split("=", 1)
            os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip('"').strip("'"))

# ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def new_session_id() -> str:
    return f"Ω-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d-%H%M')}"

def session_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return SESSIONS_DIR / f"{sid}.json"

def save_session(sid: str, history: list, state: dict):
    data = {
        "session_id": sid,
        "created_at": state.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
        "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "messages": history,
        "state": state,
    }
    path = session_path(sid)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load_session(sid: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    path = session_path(sid)
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_sessions() -> list:
    sessions = []
    for p in sorted(SESSIONS_DIR.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:20]:
        try:
            d = json.loads(p.read_text())
            sessions.append(d)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return sessions

# ── Git context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def git_info() -> str:
    try:
        branch = subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True
        ).strip()
        dirty = subprocess.call(
            ["git", "diff", "--quiet"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
        ) != 0
        return f"{branch}{'*' if dirty else ''}"
    except Exception:
        return ""

# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_banner(session_id: str):
    console.print()
    # Colorshifting banner — cycles through user's terminal colors
    colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", "#f97316", "#eab308", "#10b981", "#22d4f5"]
    banner_lines = [

        "██╔══██╗    ██║    ██║   ██╔════╝    ╚██╗ ██╔╝",
        "██████╔╝    ██║ █╗ ██║   █████╗       ╚████╔╝ ",
        "██╔══██╗    ██║███╗██║   ██╔══╝        ╚██╔╝  ",
        "██║  ██║    ╚███╔███╔╝   ██║            ██║   ",
        "╚═╝  ╚═╝    ╚══╝╚══╝    ╚═╝            ╚═╝   ",
    ]
    for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):

    cwd_str = f"  [muted]cwd:[/muted][dim]{Path.cwd().name}[/dim]"

    console.print(
        f"  [gold]R · W · Ϝ · Y[/gold]   [{mode_color}]{mode_label}[/{mode_color}]"
        f"  [muted]session:[/muted][dim]{session_id}[/dim]{cwd_str}{git_str}"
    )
    console.print(
        "  [muted]/help for commands  ·  /new to clear  ·  ctrl+c to exit  ·  "
        "!cmd to run shell[/muted]"
    )
    console.print()

# ── Response state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

last_state: dict = {}

# ── Streaming chat ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def stream_response(user: str, history: list, inject_context: str = "") -> str:
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "x-omega-operator-token": token,
    }
    msg = f"{inject_context}\n\n{user}".strip() if inject_context else user
    payload = {"user": msg, "history": history, "voiceMode": False}

    full_response = ""

    with console.status("[muted]connecting…[/muted]", spinner="dots",
                        spinner_style="#a855f7") as status:
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120) as client:
                async with client.stream(
                    "POST", f"{API_BASE}/api/chat",
                    headers=headers, json=payload,
                ) as resp:
                    # Capture response headers for /state
                    global last_state
                    last_state = {
                        "mode":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Mode", "?"),
                        "provider":   resp.headers.get("X-Provider", "?"),
                        "risk":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Risk-Score", "?"),
                        "outcome":    resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Outcome", "?"),
                        "session_id": resp.headers.get("X-Session-Id", "?"),
                        "envelope":   resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Envelope-Version", "?"),
                        "phylactery": resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Phylactery-Head", "?"),
                        "stack":      resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Stack", "?"),
                    }

                    if resp.status_code != 200:
                        body = await resp.aread()
                        body_text = body.decode()
                        status.stop()

                        # Better error diagnostics
                        if resp.status_code == 500:
                            console.print(f"\n[red]  error 500: internal server error[/red]")
                            payload_size = len(json.dumps(payload))
                            if payload_size > 100000:  # ~100KB
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  hint: request size ~{payload_size//1024}KB (too large)[/yellow]")
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  try: split your prompt into smaller pieces[/yellow]")
                            else:
                                console.print(f"[yellow]  hint: {body_text[:200]}[/yellow]")
                        else:
                            console.print(f"\n[red]  error {resp.status_code}: {body_text[:200]}[/red]\n")
                        return ""

                    status.update("[muted]synthesizing…[/muted]")

                    buf = ""
                    async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                        buf += chunk
                        while "\n" in buf:
                            line, buf = buf.split("\n", 1)
                            if line.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                                continue
                            if line.strip():
                                full_response += line + "\n"
                    if buf.strip() and not buf.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                        full_response += buf

        except httpx.ConnectError:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  connection error — {API_BASE} unreachable[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except httpx.TimeoutException:
            status.stop()
            console.print("\n[red]  request timed out[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except Exception as e:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  error: {e}[/red]\n")
            return ""

    if not full_response.strip():
        return ""

    _render_response(full_response.strip(), history=history)
    return full_response.strip()


def _render_response(text: str, history: list = None):
    console.print()
    is_markdown = any(m in text for m in ("**", "##", "```", "\n- ", "\n* ", "\n1. ", "> "))
    if is_markdown:
        console.print(Panel(
            Markdown(text),
            border_style="#2a2826",
            padding=(0, 2),
            title="[omega]Ω[/omega]",
            title_align="left",
        ))
    else:
        # Wrap long plain text
        console.print(f"[omega]Ω ›[/omega]  {text}")
    console.print()

    # Show smart suggestions (if available and history provided)
    if utils and history:
        try:
            suggestions = utils.get_smart_suggestions(text, history)
            if suggestions:
                console.print("[dim]Suggestions:[/dim]")
                for i, s in enumerate(suggestions, 1):
                    console.print(
                        f"  {i}. [{s['type']}] {s['label']:<30} "
                        f"[dim]— {s['description']}[/dim]"
                    )
                console.print()
        except Exception:
            pass  # Suggestions are optional

# ── Shell command execution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def run_shell_command(cmd: str) -> bool:
    """Execute a bang-prefixed command with risk gate. Returns True if executed."""
    risk = classify_risk(cmd)

    if risk == "high":
        console.print(f"\n[high]  ⚠  HIGH RISK:[/high] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]This operation is destructive. Continue? [y/N][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer != "y":
            console.print("  [muted]  blocked.[/muted]\n")
            return False
    elif risk == "standard":
        console.print(f"\n[std]  ▸ standard:[/std] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]Execute? [Y/n][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer == "n":
            console.print("  [muted]  skipped.[/muted]\n")
            return False

    console.print()
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True,
                                capture_output=False)
        if result.returncode != 0:
            console.print(f"  [muted]exit code {result.returncode}[/muted]")
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]  shell error: {e}[/red]")
    console.print()
    return True

# ── Slash commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def cmd_help():
    console.print()
    console.print(Rule("[muted]commands[/muted]", style="#2a2826"))
    rows = [
        ("/new",           "clear conversation history"),

        ("/files [path]",  "browse file tree"),

        ("",               ""),

    ]
    t = Table(box=None, padding=(0, 2), show_header=False)

        t.add_row(cmd, desc)
    console.print(t)
    console.print(Rule(style="#2a2826"))
